Developers
I have been informed — something is terribly wrong in the village of Garner Hills.
Indeed, that’s what my spies have indicated… or rather, what their silence has. I’ve lost contact with all of them in that region.
But one informant made it out. Reports… strangeness. Says the fires there — they don’t burn out. Not even with water. They just keep burning — endlessly. This demands investigation.
Garner Hills lies beyond our jurisdiction. It falls under the Temple of the Sun’s Protectorate.
All the more reason to investigate, and perhaps… to extend our influence.
I’ll send a scout. I have just the man. Gunnar. Good rider. Long distances, no complaints. And young enough not to be spooked by a few cursed flames.
We stand at th’ edge o’ it, m’lord. This be th’ place.
I am no lord — just a mere scout, commanded to confirm whether the rumors of an “unstoppable” fire in your village are true.

To us simple folk, all o’ you’re lords an’ ladies. Makes things easier, don’t it? But th’ rumors are true, th’ fire’s been burnin’ fer days, layin’ waste to homes. We’ve tried both water an’ magic, but nothin’ seems t’ work.
Not even magic, you say? That’s unusual.
Could it be the feyfolk? They’re elementalists and should be handy with fire, so the connection draws itself.
Are the necromancers raiding you again?
Have you ever had disasters like this before?
We’re relatively far from the Murmurwoods, m’lord. But aye, the truth is, we’re stuck between the lands o’ fey an’ Alvar, an’ folk here don’t trust outsiders.
Yet you get a minotaur to help you. Ironic, isn’t it?
If you don’t have fey living around, it’s unlikely to be them.
If you shun outsiders, there’s no wonder someone decided to pay you back. There is little to enjoy in being an outcast.
Aye, I’d say it is. But when ye’re desperate, ye don’t turn away help, even from… yer kind.
Aye, m’lord, we’ve no feyfolk here.
As ye say, m’lord.
They always come, m’lord. But never like this — with fire.
Don’t underestimate their craftiness.
You have a point. Fires and dead bodies don’t make a very good match.
We’ve had fires, but nothin’ like this! This ain’t no common fire — it’s cursed!
An’ truth be told, m’lord, we are not concerned with how it began or who’s to blame. We just want our homes an’ lives back to what they were.
I’m afraid, if it has been going on already for a while, as you say, there is not much of an actual village to get back. But let’s have a look around and see what we find.
Hm. There was a bridge here, wasn’t there? How long has it been gone?
When I set out to fetch ye, it were still standin’. So it’s fresh, I’d wager.
Doesn’t smell burnt. Just broken.
Right enough, sir. Looks snapped clean — like it’s been done deliberate‑like.
Hah. The spy wasn’t wrong about the Temple activity here.
We will need some wood to patch this up. I’ve got to see what’s on the other side.
We need more wood to fix this.
[30 Wood removed]
There. That should do it. Let’s find out what’s waiting for us across the way.

The air is filled with an oddly uncomfortable but somehow hypnotizing mixture of buzzing and whispering. It is difficult to understand whether the creature is trying to communicate with you or the noise is simply produced by its wings cutting through the air.
What is this? I’ve never seen anything like it. Never heard anything like it, either.
Ask me no questions, m’lord. Ye be the one chosen t’ put an end t’ these cursed flames.
Speaking of the flames… The creature looks rather fiery, so it might as well be connected to the whole issue.
To be precise, I was chosen to scout the area and report back. Never have I agreed to be your village’s savior.
Thanks for lettin’ me know, m’lord.
So that fiery insect was not the only one.
I wonder if these creatures are the cause of the fire or they are simply attracted by it.
Perhaps we should try to capture it and study it further in Alvar. Let me approach it —

As soon as you try approaching the creature, it lets out an intimidating and furious screech reminiscent of tearing a piece of fabric — a sound that seems to rip through the air, as if reality itself is being shredded.
Fine. If it’s a fight it wants, it’s a fight it’ll get.
What’s this sound?
Sound, m’lord?
Can’t you hear it? It’s like… a song. An odd song.
I’ve never heard anything like it before.
You… you don’t need another hat! You already… already… You have a hat, why another hat?
Need? No, no, no, but… want? Yes. Yes. Yes…
Look at those two! Seems like some lich forgot their puppets here, and now their magic is fading. What’s going on here?
Tell him! Tell him! Make him give it! Give what? Give it! Give the hat to me!
He’s got a hat! Already. Me — need it. I’d look so pretty in it! You’re big… Big! Tell him to give it! It — I… Wait, who are you?!
You already have a hat. Why do you need another one?
Actually, it looks pretty on him. Leave him be.
I think I know how to solve your dilemma. I’m taking the hat for myself.
I… found it? I’m wearing it! Why give to Skelton when Bonehart is stronger… stronger than… uh, Skelton?
Finders keepers. That’s fair. Keep it for yourself.
So you don’t need it. Give it to Skelton. He does.
Since might makes right for you, it’s only fair that I take it.
Told you! Told you, told you, told you!
Fine.
Thanks, oh thanks! Thanks for the… what was it? Oh yes! Thanks for the charity! Isn’t it wonderful to feel charitable?
No!
Noooo!
Ha! See!
Whatever… I’ll just… steal it later. Wait, what am I stealing later?..
Nooooo!
Over here!
You’re one of the Silvertongue’s spies, aren’t you? What are you doing here? He was complaining that he hadn’t heard from you in a while.

Typical Caspair — always impatient. That templar down the way made sure I couldn’t send a word. When the fire trouble began, the humans sealed off the village from the north — that’s where the fire came from.
But before I could slip away, they posted another patrol here on the south side. Thanks for dealing with it.
Speaking of the trouble, what exactly happened here?
It’s strange. All fires — torches, ovens, even candles — became eternal. Neither water nor spells worked. Chaos erupted. The peasants panicked, which drew in the knights. And here we are.
So the blaze just spread on its own because it couldn’t be extinguished?
I can’t be certain. It all started so fast. I’ve had no time to gather proper intelligence.
But listen: there are other Alvar agents in the village. Keep your eyes open. They might have found something I haven’t.
Now, I’ve got to make it back to Alvar before Silvertongue sends a search party or decides I’m expendable. See you in the shadows, minotaur.
What’s that sound?..
Mighty horns of my grandpa, what on Enroth was that?! Are you alright?
By Chalice’s grace, we are now! We don’t really know ourselves. That… that swarm just appeared from nowhere. It nearly took us all.

I was ready to feel the warm embrace of the Sun, but you saved us. After my house was reduced to ash, I have little left. Here, take this coin. My grandmother swore she got it from Arina herself! Said it brings luck.
You sure about that? You don’t seem very lucky to me.
But I’m alive, thanks to you! That’s luck enough for me. Take it — it’s all I have to give.
[Accept]
[Refuse]
[You gained the Last Coin artifact!]
Well, thanks then. I’ll keep it safe.
No need to thank me. It’s Alvar’s duty to protect each and every one in Jadame.
Then may the Sun shine upon your path, warrior of Alvar. I’ll never forget the day you saved my life.
What?! Halt! Identify yourself!
Gunnar, son of Gunnar. We’re here on the orders of the Triumvirate, scouting the region for answers.
Answers? There’s nothing here that concerns Alvarian spies! Garner Hills answers to the Temple of the Sun. Stay out of it.
And explain how you crossed the river. The bridge has been destroyed.
Ah, so you did break it. Convenient. We’re no spies, Captain. Jadame is a land of free nations. Every power has the right to know about threats to the continent.
There’s no “threat”! At least… We are still figuring that out. Until then, no one enters or leaves the village. That’s final. Unless… unless you have special permission.
Special permission, eh? Let’s see if I have it in my purse.
My axe is my special permission. Have a look. [Attack]
What’s that? The Provost’s lucky token?! What have you done to him?!
[Tell him what happened]
What I ha — You think I’m a thief?! How dare you, human! [Attack]
He’s fine. He was cornered by… well, this might sound insane, but fiery cicadas or hoppers. Whatever they were, my axe sorted them out.
Afterward, your Provost gave me this trinket. Said his nan got it from Arina herself. Drivel if you ask me, but that’s his story.
From Arina herself, you say? Huh, that does sound like our Provost. Fine, Gunnar, son of Gunnar. You and your people may pass. But don’t make me regret this.
snorts Alvarians. At least you know how to grease the wheels. Fine, 2,500 gold.
Here you go. [Give 2500 Gold]
Well, I don’t have such money.
On second thought… Never mind. [Attack]
You may pass. But tread lightly.
Then stop wasting my time and get out.
I see. May the Forefathers be my witnesses, I’ve tried to do it the polite way.
Garrison, to arms!
Intruder! Seize him!
Huh? What is this? The ground is burning… like it’s alive. Has it always been this way?
Not that I recall, no.
Fascinating… if not dreadful…
Another patch of scorched ground… I think we are almost there.
Let our graceful blades join your cause. For Alvar!
[The Onyx Dancers joined your army]
Wonder what happened here. Let’s find out, yeah?
[The Infiltrators joined your army]
Point us to some action, will you? We were getting bored.
[The Onyx Dancers joined your army]
We acknowledge your authority.
[The Infiltrators joined your army]
To be back in Alvar… Sigh. Let’s get this mission over with.
[The Onyx Dancers joined your army]
We’ve been told to expect you, and we are ready to assist.
Temple patrols lurk everywhere, vigilant and unyielding. Yet behind one of the villages, we discovered a concealed pathway — a secret passage to slip past their main forces unnoticed.
[The Infiltrators joined your army]
Halt! This territory is under lockdown by order of the governor of Sunspire. No one enters or leaves.
Charming greeting. I’ve got my orders too, straight from the Triumvirate. I’m here to scout and report on the delightful disaster you’ve got brewing. Not fight.
Orders or not, the governor’s decree stands. This area is closed to all outsiders. Report that back to your Triumvirate.
Templar, let’s not waste time. You move your squad out of the way, we move on, and we both forget that this little chat ever happened. What do you say?
I say, the area is clo —
Enough! If you insist on wasting my time, I’ll clear the path myself.
Ugh… That was not something I expected to find here.
Anyway, I have to report these insects to the Triumvirate immediately.
Let me summarize, then: eternal flames, aggressive Templars and —
Sentient insects. Or at least highly unusual ones. I couldn’t capture any — but they are no ordinary bugs. They are burning, too.
Noted. Thank you for the report. You are dismissed.
Now, then… What do we make of all this?
[Discuss strange fire]
[Discuss situation in the village]
Troubling. Magical flames are one thing, but fully inextinguishable? In a backwater village where maybe a fifth of the population can even read? This warrants concern.
We need input from the druids of the Murmurwoods. Either their elementalists are responsible, or they may know who is.

We had an outpost in that region once. The elf agents stationed there were obsessed with reporting “unusual magical activity” every time a fey sneezed. It became insufferable, so I temporarily shut it down.
Now, it may be prudent to reopen those inquiries.
The most straightforward path is to address the Temple of the Sun first. They’ve already stationed knights there; surely, they’ve been filing reports, or at least keeping records.
Besides, they’re already entangled in a separate conflict with the necromancers.
Aiding the humans could strengthen our diplomatic ties with them.
Or — hear me out — we could offer our help to the necromancers and see what the Guild knows.
Templars are zealous and quick to label anything a heresy, but the Guild is far more methodical. They might have insight into this fiery insect phenomenon… something the Temple wouldn’t.
If we assist the Guild, the Temple won’t share any information with us afterwards.
And the same goes for siding with the Temple. But imagine this: the necromancers would owe us a favor. And let’s not forget, their influence is growing — rapidly.

So if we look at our options, we could try to contact the elementalists of the Murmurwoods and, beyond that, pursue information from either the Temple or the necromancers — both of whom are currently busy tearing each other apart.
This calls for careful deliberation…

Hmph. Haven’t set my hoof in the Murmurwoods for ages. Hopefully I won’t find too much trouble here. Feyfolk should be less bothersome than the templars, but I doubt they’ll exactly be thrilled by the presence of an Alvarian emissary either.
The Murmurwoods and the Dominion of Alvar never did get along too well. To these plant‑lovers, we are too “organized.” Whatever that’s supposed to mean…
Soldiers! We are going into the woods! Let’s make sure we do not waste the Triumvirate’s time.
Thank the Forefathers, someone survived! My name is Gunnar, and I’m —
Let me go! You hurt me! It feels like crystals of flame are burning my skin!
Whoa, easy there! We didn’t mean to scare or harm you.
You seem as confused as everyone else in these woods. Is this about the explosion in that town?
Explosion! The crash was horrible… an irreversible mistake that wasn’t meant to be! Fire! Vapor! Smoke…
Wait, was it you that killed everyone in Silverwood?
You’re not making any sense… None of this place does. I don’t want to do this, but you are leaving me no choice. [Attack]
I don’t want to hurt you. But I need you to pull yourself together and tell me — what do you know about the fires, or the explosion in that town?
Yes!!! Y… yes. We… I…

The figure convulses as cracks spread across its form, spilling bursts of light. Energy surges wildly, flashing brilliant one moment and plunging into shadow the next. A raw, anguished sound escapes, shaking the ground. You can hear stones crashing in the distance.
We… were meant to…

The figure convulses as cracks spread across its form, spilling bursts of light. Energy surges wildly, flashing brilliant one moment and plunging into shadow the next. A raw, anguished sound escapes, shaking the ground. You can hear stones crashing in the distance.
Stop it! I just want to know what happened here!
A horrible mistake… set me on fire! Misplacement…
I’m not great with riddles. Is there another way to explain what’s tormenting you?
Set you on fire? But you… you’re some kind of fire yourself, aren’t you?
Tethered! Roots… Tethered.
Tethered? You’re tethered? To the roots? What does that even mean?
Put this… person in chains! Looks like I have no choice but to figure out what’s tethering them.
Something about the roots? The Murmurwoods’ roots are all interconnected underground.
Maybe if I find where they surface, I could untangle this mess.
Curse my hooves! It seems the mental distress of this odd duck has blocked the only path back to the outpost.
By the Forefathers’ mighty chins, this place has been torn to smithereens! They’re all dead…
Then again, does anyone really know what happens to feyfolk when they… well, when they’re no more?
No matter. I can see traces — someone’s still here. We will find them.
Strange fire… Cannot be extinguished… Summon a higher elemental to investigate…
Fire? Or Water?
As they say in Garrote Gorge… why not both?
Brrr. What was that? Some kind of… ghost?
It felt like a specter passed right through me. Could it be that the Mycelium preserves not just the bodies of the dead, but their memories too?
From what I could gather, the druids already knew about the strange fire and tried to solve the mystery… But whatever they did went horribly wrong.
Of course, we can hold two summoning rites at the same time!
A trivial affair…
A piercing scream erupts, sharp and raw, like shards of shattered glass drilling into your mind alone. An anguished echo of rage and despair twists your nerves, drowning out all else.
Hngh! Ugh… That was… utterly unpleasant.
So, they tried summoning two elementals at once? Clearly didn’t end well. Looks like they got… one? And a broken one.
Not broken! We joined! We… joined.
Huh, you are sounding a bit more coherent now, friend. One more tether to go, and then maybe — just maybe — I can drag you back to Alvar without looking like a crank.
The roots… damn it, they’re binding them together!
This isn’t right! The Mycelium is behaving like… it is sick. The Murmurwoods are sick!
Oh no, no, no! They are colliding! The summons… RUN!
We… am… here.
Cutting through the Mycelium seems to have brought you back to your senses.
My name is Gunnar. I’m an envoy from Alvar. Who are you?
La… Fi… I’m —
We are Lafiur. We are… thankful? To you.
You are? Good, now that you can talk, explain what’s going on here.

We know not. Lafiur knows not… The Whispering World. The World beyond the world. The source of all elements, our home, where the root‑paths grow into. The root‑paths spark onto this… into this place! We are a spark!
A spark? I have no idea what you’re saying. Any better explanation?
So you are not like other elementals, right?
We have no idea either! We are Lafiur!
That and this! We are Lafiur!
I’m not skilled enough to deal with this… Why are you so strange? Like… not fully here?
Hm… The root‑paths got clogged!
Root‑paths? You mean the Mycelium? Is it clogged? By what?
We Lafiur know not. Knot tied down there. And… smoke. Fire! Hot fire!
Smoke and “hot fire”? So… unnatural burning happening to the Murmurwoods’ roots as well? Underground, is that what you mean?
And those druids… they summoned you. And you are —
…not an elemental, are you?
…one of those elementals, are you? But which one, the Fire or the Water one?
No idea. I hope you can remember more, because you are coming along.
You’re two elementals that got entangled.
We are! Lafiur are together!
You are coming with me. Let the bigwigs sort this out.
The scouting troop from Alvar! You there, I need some backup!
You again?
What mess have you gotten yourself into this time?
Not my fault! The other spies have gone mad, and I’m outnumbered!
Stay out of this, scout. Let Caspair’s lackey fend for himself. Unless…
Unless what? You’re threatening me now? Bold talk for someone cornering an ally like a common thug. What are you, some would‑be revolutionary?
What’s gotten into those guys? Sure, Caspair can be a pain in the horns, but this is a first — his own agents turning on each other. Oh, right — Gunnar.
Thanks for stepping in, Gunnar. About your question… I’ve got some thoughts, but I need to confirm them first. I’ll see you back in Alvar.
[You gained the Spyglass artifact!]
Hmm… That’s not how I remember these crystal mines. Sure, I’ve heard of veins mixed with other materials, but…
These look like hollow shells. Probably useless in this state… but there’s something odd about this.
Is this… blood? No, it doesn’t seem right. Too unnatural.
It’s as if the color itself is just… floating there.
Strange… yet oddly beautiful.
[Touch the color red]
[Drink the color red]
[Stomp it into the ground]
Hello? Who’s there?
It’s… gone.
A communion…
Hello? Who are you?
To act without knowing… how bold.
Huh?! Show yourself!
It’s… gone.
I… feel strange. Almost like the world’s colors shifted — red’s become brighter…
The blob of color shatters and dissipates into nothingness.
That’s… unsettling. But I have the oddest feeling this was the right choice.
Mrrow!
What’s wrong, little guy?
Mrow! Mrrrow! Mreeeeeee!
Oh, I get it — it’s the crystals, isn’t it? I’ve heard you little furballs have a taste for them. But not these strange, colorless ones, huh? I wonder what’s happened to them.
Mrek!
I know, buddy, it’s tough out here. But we’ve got places to be.
How about this — I’ll give you these tasty crystals, and you let me through. Deal? [Give 15 Crystals]
How about this — I get you some tasty crystals, and then you let me through. Deal?
Sorry, buddies, but I need to get through. [Attack]
The kittenhorns gobble up the crystals eagerly and then surround you with happy purrs.
[The Kittenhorns joined your army]
Not what I expected, but alright. Here, kitty — let’s get moving.
Mrrrrr!
Here you go, little ones. Something tasty for you. [Give 15 Crystals]
I’m on it! Wait here a bit longer. [Leave]
Sorry, buds, but I will have those crystals to myself. We’ll have to do it the hard way. [Attack]
Maybe I’ll think a bit more. [Leave]
The kittenhorns excitedly devour the crystals.
[The Kittenhorns joined your army]
Not what I expected, but alright. Here, kitty — let’s get moving.
Mrrrrr! Rrrrr!
I’m on it! Wait here a bit longer. [Leave]
Sorry, buds, but I can’t waste time running your errands. We’ll have to do this the hard way. [Attack]
What in the depths are you supposed to be?
You again! Are you following us or something?
…And there it goes.
We observe. Higher directives govern our purpose. Do not interfere.
And whose authority is higher than the Triumvirate’s?
Who — or what — are you, exactly?
And what is your purpose?
We shall leave now. Do not interfere.
Oh no, you are not leaving to report to… whoever it is you answer to. [Attack]
Wait! If you are watching everything, you must know something about the strange fires and those fiery insects! What can you tell me?
We do not provide answers. We serve balance. You shall hear from us again.
Troops! Stand down! That’s a lair of infernal hydras up ahead. Didn’t expect them to dwell here.
Typically, these hydras have a taste for crystals. Back in the day, we used to tame them with those. Maybe I can scrounge some up nearby…
The hydra coils its massive, scaled body, several heads snapping towards you in unison. Its central maw splits open, releasing a deep, guttural snarl. The air shimmers with heat.
Here, do you want to try these? [Give 30 Crystals]
Maybe I will need those crystals somewhere else…
The hydra’s heads snap at the offering, crunching the crystals between serrated teeth. A low, rumbling purr echoes through the air.
[The Infernal Hydras joined your army]
Perfect. They’ll definitely come in handy.
A thunderous, rumbling roar shakes the cavern, echoing like fire crackling through dry wood.
I get it, I get it. Staying out of your way.
Mreow!
Oh, another one! Here, take these. [Give 10 Crystals]
Sorry, buds, but I will keep these crystals to myself. We’ll have to do it the hard way. [Attack]
[Leave them be]
Purrr!
[The Kittenhorns joined your army]
Mrrreow!
Mrrrreow!
Mrrrrreow!
Mreow?
I don’t have time for this. [Attack]
Let me see what I can find. I’ll be back.
Mmmu… Murr... Murr… Murr… Wood…
All the crystals have faded. These mines are of no use.
Hm… this is unusual.
I know Caspair shut the outpost down, yet it looks like someone took every stone with them before leaving.
Unfortunately, we’ll need reinforcements to scout this area safely. Let’s look for any nearby Alvarian dwellings. Surely some of the former inhabitants must still be lingering around here.
Gunnar! What a surprise! Haven’t seen you for ages!
Always a pleasure — wait. Seen?
Hah! You caught me! One of your men shouted your name, figured I’d run with it.
Smart. Look, we need you and your lot to help us re‑establish that outpost.
We’re in! It’s not like we’ve had much to do since Silvertongue shut the place down.
Alvar diruil. Glory to Alvar! I’m re‑establishing the local outpost, and we need your help. Mind joining us?
We’ve been expecting you. Caspair sent word ahead that you’d come. We’ve already made preparations.
Good. Do you know why the place looks… half‑destroyed?
Not a clue. Our squad was transferred long before Caspair closed the place down.
I see… A mystery for another time, then. Soldiers, give them a hand!
Good da —
Yes, yes. The outpost. We know. The relocation is already underway.
Efficient as ever. It’s always a pleasure dealing with the spies.
That should do it.
Now that everyone has returned to the outpost, maybe we’ll finally be able to build something useful there.
What in Jadame is this beast supposed to be? Is it even conscious? They don’t lie when they say that everything in the Murmurwoods is interconnected. I just didn’t expect it to be this literal…
I am Gunnar, envoy of the Triumvirate. I come to talk to the local elementalists to get any information on the recent outburst of fires and —
The agony! Of excruciating pain, tearing the fabric of our continuum. Piercing into the depth of our mind…
You sound unwell… Can we help you somehow?
Very poetic, but can you actually show us the way?
Oh, my. We don’t have time for this. Just let us pass.
…the agony!
Greetings! Finally a faun, so —
The agony… The roots… tangled… The woods are… crushing…
I’ve heard something similar before. Didn’t end well…
I wonder what’s torturing them.
This is unlike anything I have heard about this place. Connection or not, why is everyone out of their minds?
Agony? Something about pain?
…lvarian in the Murmurwoods. Yet you speak as if you’re one of us.
Wait — you are sane! And intact!
But… Minotaurs are not connected to the Mycelium… A trick, then… a trick of my mind.
No! Wait! I’m real — an actual minotaur! You’re not daydreaming!
Or worse… an apparition of evil…
Oh, damn it all!
The woods grow redder up ahead.
The good news? We’re probably heading in the right direction.
The bad? “Right” could easily mean “mortally dangerous.”
Halt! This area has been claimed by the Temple of the Sun.
Claimed? Under whose authority does the Temple just waltz in and “claim” an area? And besides — why here?
These mines are the only ones in the region with proper crystals left. The rest… they’ve turned pale, almost like empty shells.
Right, I’ve seen it too.
Now that I think of it, I’ve encountered some liquid exactly the same color as those crystals. Made me hear strange things.
I’ve seen what’s draining these crystals — wasp‑like creatures, feeding on the color. Have you?
Sorry, folks, but I need those crystals to feed some very angry kittenhorns.
What’s going on in these parts, anyway? The feyfolk I’ve encountered so far seemed really disturbed by something.
I’ve no idea what you’re talking about.
Come to think of it… why is your Temple so interested in crystals all of a sudden? You don’t use them in your rites.
We use everything.
It is obvious the Temple is hiding something, and I will not let it interfere! [Attack]
If you say so. Keep your secrets and your crystals. For now. [Leave]
We have a clear order to protect the mines!
It’s strange, isn’t it? Never seen anything like it. On the bright side, it means crystals will probably be worth a fortune soon. But make no mistake — these are already ours.
Aren’t you worried about the feyfolk? Seems like every native I’ve encountered here recently has turned completely mad.
Suuure… Have fun guarding pretty stones while the place is going mad. [Leave]
I suppose that’s as good a reason as any to “claim” this place for Alvar. [Attack]
They all sound the same to me. Why would we be concerned?
Not concerned, huh? Even in regular woods, that’d be reckless behavior. And we’re standing here in the Murmurwoods, where every blade of grass whispers to the rest of the forest.
Aren’t you curious, at least? Don’t you find all this strange?
Trust an Alvarian: There is a cause for concern.
This is heretical nonsense! Take your fairy tales elsewhere. We are under the Sun’s blessing, and we need none of these superstitions.
New to Jadame, huh? You’ve got much to learn. But fine — enjoy your “blessing.” I’ll leave you to it. [Leave]
Blessing or not, taking fey land just because you got greedy makes this plain old malfeasance. [Attack]
We’re soldiers. Our orders leave no time for idle curiosity. We’re here to do our duty, nothing more.
Sigh. Fine, enjoy guarding your claim. I hope it’s worth it. [Leave]
That kind of blind obedience? It always leads to disaster. Let me “reclaim” these mines for Alvar’s sake — and until this strangeness is resolved. [Attack]
I’ve reconsidered…
…Nevermind. [Leave]
…Alvar actually needs these crystals more than you do. [Attack]
You’re the same kind of creature that turned Garner Hills into an inferno, aren’t you?
I’d like to think you’re harmless, but I saw what your friends did to those peasants.
Crystals… you eat them too, don’t you? Though not like kittenhorns, no.
You drink them.
I wonder if you also hear some strange voices when you do it…
Can you actually talk?
Soldiers! Let’s try and capture this one. [Attack]
The hornet buzzes with menace in response.
We’ll leave you be. [Leave]
Soldiers! Let’s try and capture this one. [Attack]
These ones seem less ferocious than whatever was roaming loose in Garner Hills.
But catching it? No luck. At least we have something to discuss back in Alvar.
I think I’m gonna capture you anyway.
We commend your report, Gunnar. Continue the investigation, and take that… Lafiur with you.
Aye!
— agreed, then, Gunnar. You brought them here, so it’s your responsibility to get them up to speed.
So, mighty one with many names!
We are partners! Like Flame and Water!
Not sure how good of partners flame and water are, but I guess that’s what we are now.
Your Triplepeople saw the flicker and flow within us!
[Correct them]
[Ignore them]
It’s “Triumvirate…” Forefathers, help me.
Ignore it, Gunnar. You can do this.
Oh, it tumbles heavy like stones. Triplepeople dances, like streams through cracks!
Huh?
Nevermind. The important part is that you’ve preserved your connection to the Mycelium and… other things. You can sense them.
Sense? We do not know this word. But we distinguish that something is disturbed in the Murmurwoods. Something is on fire.
Well, your distinguishing ability seemed useful enough to the Triumvirate, and now it’s our job to investigate. If you sense the fire, my guess is we are on the right track.
However, that part of the Murmurwoods is in conflict. The Temple is fighting feyfolk for territory, and we’ll need to pick a side if we venture there.
Why do they fight? Are they also stuck together, like us?
Ehm. No, not exactly. The Templars are on some — how did they put it? — “resource‑claiming mission.” But this mission has gone on longer than the feyfolk can tolerate.

Truthfully, we don’t have all the details. But since we have to enter the depths of the Murmurwoods, our safest bet is to contact the Temple and hope they’ll back us up if we get in trouble with the fey.
Why would the woods‑dwellers trouble us?
Because we serve the Dominion of Alvar. And the Dominion has had many years of disagreements with the Murmurwoods. So if we clash with them, I’d rather do it with Temple support.
Clash?.. No! We do not wish harm upon the growing creatures! We breathe in unison! If we must pickaside, let’s pickaside the Woods!
Out of the question! Listen, you got me into this whole mess with the forest. I have enough on my hands dealing with you — I am not adding a whole faction of fey to the mix!
But what if we…
…The tax collector who went to Garner Hills was called Valentina. She was supposed to be back here by now. A scout from her entourage came back but left without giving her report.
Does that answer your questions? We could really use Alvar’s assistance against the undead.
Not all of them, no. Finding this scout is far more critical than your dispute with the necromancers. But if they get in our way, they can argue with my axe. Where was the scout last seen?
She stayed briefly before heading into Guild territory. We don’t know why, and our resources are stretched thin. You might still catch her.
Hmm. Why are the necromancers attacking you? Doesn’t the Dusk Treaty dictate, “Neither side shall raise their blade against the other”?
How convenient.

We’ve learned they are preparing a foul ritual near our borders. How could we let them perform it?! After they agreed that no magic of the dead shall be performed in the proximity to any holy Temple grounds!
I thought the Necromancers Guild had rules against this sort of abuse.
Sounds like they’ve gone off the deep end. I’ll see what we can do.
I don’t know why you would expect them to follow any rules.
Thank you. We’ll appreciate any aid you can provide.
Before you leave — there’s an abbey west of here. Disturbing rumors have reached us: the priestess there has vanished. However, we can’t waste any more squads on investigation.
Hmph. If saving your own people is a “waste,” don’t expect Alvar to do your work for you.
We were heading that way in any case. We’ll check on your missing holy ladies as well.
You! Leave at once — these are Lord Galthran’s lands, and he is not to be disturbed!
Who is Lord Galthran, and why did he break the Dusk Treaty? What’s the point of all this bloodshed?
Why are you here? Isn’t the Dusk Treaty still in force? And now it looks like you are just about to break it.
We don’t have time for this — let our blades speak! [Attack]
Break the treaty? Hah, what a farce. First, the lightwarts pillage our lands, and now they can’t even bother to tell their mercenaries the truth. Pathetic!
Grrrmmph. I will have you know that we are Alvar envoys, mage. One last time: why are you here? Choose your next words carefully.
We’re not hired by the Temple — the Triumvirate sent us to investigate strange happenings. Lower your weapons, and we may discuss it.
Poor choice of words. [Attack]
There’s no point. You mercenaries will say anything to earn more gold. Once you’re a part of my army, you can ponder the reasons… or not.
Whoever your lord is, diplomacy isn’t his strength, given your disdain for it.
No matter. If I can’t get answers from you peacefully, I’ll get them after I’m done with you.
Let’s see if your might matches your arrogance!
Enough! Stop your meddling — Lord Galthran’s plans must come to fruition!
Haven’t I killed you already?
Dying is merely a setback for servants of my sire — I’ll come back as many times as I must to stop you.
Hmm. I wish you knew when to stop fighting and start talking.
We are just wasting our people here…
Your numbers are limited, whilst mi —
No more words — only the song of clashing steel. Face us again!
Our promise to Sunspire is fulfilled. And at last, we’re free from that necromancer’s endless prattle.
Now, to sort out the scout situation…
That looks like Temple scout armor. Aides, have a look at her. Could she be the one we’ve been looking for?
Blast it! She’s dead.
Thankfully, not irreversible. Her soul hasn’t yet vacated her mortal coil.
And you are…?
You’re a necromancer, aren’t you? You aren’t really helping to dispel the rumors about your people: A person dies and, here you are.
You’re a boneseeker aren’t you? Before you begin to take her away for the rites, could you tell me how she died?
My name’s Klastor, but that is none of your concern. I’m not a combatant, if that’s what you’re wondering, and neither did I kill her.

Actually, I’m more of a boneseeker. I find corpses and deliver them to actual necromancers. Concerning the “rumors”… you work for Triumvirate, don’t you? There are also rumors that your superiors would do anything for power. But I’m not judging you by your friends. You know, just saying.
Not by my hand, if that’s what you’re intending.
There’s a conflict going on. It tends to kill people.
I take it you had some business with the lady?
Yes. My mission was to question her. Learn about her last assignment.
Well, thank your godlings — or whoever you believe in — that you have met me!
I can resurrect her, so you may question her. Temporarily, and for a price, and she won’t be happy. But you’ll get what you need.
What do you mean, “won’t be happy”?
Well… Humans that serve the Temple are never happy to receive the gift of undeath. Something about the soul joining the sun or similar nonsense. Good thing we ain’t asking, right?
I need to question her. What payment do you want?
If this goes against her beliefs so greatly, I don’t want to resurrect her. Surely there must be another way?
I’ve heard there’s an artifact called Shard of Serenity hidden somewhere around. Bring it to me, and you’ll get to have a word with the girl.
All right. We’ll be back with the artifact. Doesn’t really matter. We’ll bring it.
Can’t I just pay enough for you to hire another courier?
That would be very generous of you, but I am not resurrecting the girl for gold. Only for the artifact. Now, start searching, or accept that she is dead for good.
It can’t ever be easy, can it?
Why bother? The Templars didn’t bother with moral dilemmas when they sent you to kill others for their own profit, did they? Or aren’t you coming from Sunspire?
We are, but what does it have to do with the scout’s death?

With death, nothing. But if she visited Sunspire, she must’ve left a report — nobody would let her leave again until she did. Means, they could’ve shared it with you, but then you wouldn’t be fighting their battles, would you?
They deceived you, and you’re too afraid to turn the tables on them.
The governor of Sunspire has clearly deceived me, but she hasn’t… You say her report is in the city, huh? I think I know someone who can steal it for me.
The governor of Sunspire has clearly deceived me, but she hasn’t… You say her report is in the city, huh? Then I’ll just find someone who can steal it for me.
I won’t say another word to those liars in Sunspire. We’re resurrecting the scout. What payment do you want?
Hah! Well, fortunate for you, I planned to stick around for a while. Let me know when you change your mind.
I’ll only ask for one artifact for my services!
Back so soon?
As I said, bring me the Shard of Serenity — and interrogate the scout all you like. This aimless nonsense is a waste of time.
Fine, I’ll get it for you.
Calm down, here is your artifact. [Give Shard of Serenity]
I’m still considering my options.
I can’t seem to get it, and I cannot waste my time figuring out why. Just resurrect the scout.
Aargh, you’re useless. Alright, I’ll deal with it myself later. Take your precious girl.
Well, that’s some good news, isn’t it? Now we can proceed!
Did you bring the Shard of Serenity?
Not yet.
Then what are you doing here? Keep searching.
Calm down, here is your artifact.
[The Shard of Serenity artifact removed from your inventory]
Well, that’s some good news, isn’t it? Now we can proceed!
We don’t have to use your services after all.
Pah. Not going to cry about it; I still have her body. Her bones will eventually benefit someone else, and not you.
At least my hands are clean.
I strongly doubt she would agree to undergo the rituals of Shadowspire! Seize him! [Attack]
Chalice, please accept this servant’s soul. Chalice, please accept this servant’s soul. Chalice —
Wait — Where am I? Who are you?
My name is Gunnar. Unfortunately, you died, but I had to disturb you. I need to ask a few questions about your last —
Disturb me?! You mean… what have you done?! I — I was so close! I could feel the Sun! Why did you drag me back? Why?!
Curse you, defiler! May the Sun turn its light away from your spirit! Die!!!
I felt the Chalice… the Sun’s warm, all‑consuming embrace… Why did you have to chain me to the wretched cage of endless existence?
I’m sorry. I regret what I’ve done.
I need you to tell me about your last mission. This goes beyond your personal fate — the entire destiny of Jadame might be in the balance.
Save your regrets! They mean nothing to me!
I understand, but I still need you to tell me about your last mission. The very destiny of Jadame could be at stake.
Is that so…?
Why hasn’t Valentina reached the village of Garner Hills?
Valentina… I served in her army. We were tasked with collecting taxes from the farmers, but we were ambushed. The medusae struck from every direction. The fire… it wouldn’t stop burning.
The fire that does not go out. I’ve seen it. It has something to do with the medusae?
They captured her… they captured Valentina…
Hm. Does not seem like she knows anything else.
More ominous mysteries. Guess we’ll be paying the Wild Ones a visit. Even if the abduction does not have anything to do with the fire, either Valentina or her captors may know something about it.
Woe… Woe to me…
You again? Don’t you have better places to be?
What are you fools doing?! To my side, now! We must stand against the breathless horde!
Abomination! Stay back!
Am I interrupting a battle… or something else entirely?
You’re interrupting betrayal. These cowards have shown their true colors as deserters, abandoning their oath!
Don’t believe it! The abomination speaks lies!
How dare you call your liege an abomination! You fear the undead and lack the courage to press on — admit it!
Those undead, they killed you. You know that, right?
You’re dead, and yet you’re still fighting to protect your men? That’s noble, if nothing else. These soldiers are fools for abandoning you over appearances.
Killed me? ’Tis but a scratch!
You are… well, undead, to be precise. That last strike wasn’t “but a scratch”; it ended your life. You just haven’t accepted it yet.
No… that’s impossible.
How could I fall and not feel the Chalice’s touch? How could my soul remain here?
Leave me. I need time to think.
Thank you. He was… not himself anymore. But it’s no simple thing to strike down your own liege.
Then why were you following him?

We weren’t following an undead creature willingly. He was our commander in life, and we swore loyalty to him. But when he fell, it seems his will lingered — and we followed out of fear and duty. I can’t explain it better than that.
There’s dark magic at work. It must be the vampire’s doing, the one attacking Sunspire. But I’m just a soldier, not a mage.
Doesn’t sound like he understood it any better than you.
Perhaps not. But he was right about one thing: we’ve become deserters.
Then follow me. Alvar’s mission here is lawful and righteous. Joining us is a way to redeem yourselves and avoid further trouble.
Then turn yourselves in. If you regret what’s happened, let your rulers decide your fate.
Thank you for a second chance, sir!
[The Swordsmen joined your army]
They will never forgive us… No, we can’t do this!
We won’t let you turn us in!
Dead? That’s absurd! I’m very much alive. That last blow was nothing — I’m still standing!
He’s wrong. He’s… something else now. His soul is twisted. We cannot follow him any longer. It is too much.
If he’s willing to keep fighting for you, shouldn’t you honor that?
No soul can be twisted beyond redemption. If he wants to fight for the cause, he can be saved.
I… am…?
We can’t trust him. He’s not the man we swore fealty to anymore.
I… will…?
Not with death magic. It consumes and corrupts. There’s no coming back from this.
You’re mad for siding with him. We won’t let this stand. Attack!
How can it be? How could I not feel the Chalice’s touch? I need… to think.
Tough break. Missing the moment of your own death must be hard to process.
Wait! Please, our sisters need help, I beg —
Calm down, ma’am. If you mean the Sun Temple priestesses captured by necromancers, they are already with me.
…Oh.
This minotaur is escorting us to the abbey! Without him, we would have suffered a terrible fate.
Our deepest gratitude to you, warriors of Alvar. We will immediately go tending to our sisters’ wounds.
May the Sun shine upon all your endeavors!
[Attack and Defense increased by 2]
Calm down, ma’am. If you mean the Sun Temple priestesses captured by necromancers, we’ve already dealt with it. They’re safe.
Truly? You saved them?
Necromancers have their rites, but so do you. No one should be forced to participate in them against their will.
I… Our gratitude is vast enough to touch the Chalice. You truly deserve its blessing!
[Attack and Defense increased by 2]
Wait! Our saviors, we beg for your help!
“Saviors”? Us?
Yes! Please, listen! Our sisters are in grave danger.
The necromancers plan to sacrifice them in one of their foul, profane rituals. They are wounded and too weak to defend themselves. You must help!
So, you’re exactly who we were looking for. Sorry, sunshine, but you’re coming with us.
What? Where — No! No you cannot! You wicked beast! Better that we die fighting you!
The necromancers plan to sacrifice them in one of their foul, profane rituals. They are wounded and too weak to defend themselves. You must help!
Sigh. I suppose we might as well.
Honestly, this sounds like a task for someone with loftier ideals.
We’re coming with you! Our magic is strong against the undead, and we will fight at your side.
[The Sun Heralds joined your army]
Worst comes to worst, we will end their lives quickly. Far better than their souls being corrupted by necromancers.
You definitely know how to brighten a day, don’t you?
Loftier ideals?! And you would abandon us in our distress? I suppose you are no true hero, then.
Not really, no. Just an agent of Alvar, doing what needs doing.
Fine. Leave, then, agent of Alvar. We will keep waiting here for someone with loftier ideals.
Don’t even think about interfering with our… service. The feeble will be purged, and those weaklings’ escape is merely a delay of their fate.
I won’t allow this. Let the priestesses go! [Attack]
The service, right. Indeed, not our business, so we will be on our way. [Leave]
If they escaped, are they really that weak? We could help you, but do mind that we are not a charity.
That is the place! Our sisters are here, about to be sacrificed in some horrid ritual!
Are you ready? The necromancers will be well prepared for intruders.
Indeed we are. Let’s put an end to their plans. [Attack]
I haven’t thought about it. Let’s come back a bit later; we need to make some preparations. [Leave]
Get away from them, filth!!
Intruders! Take them! Don’t let the priestesses escape!
And what are these agents of Alvar doing in our land? Sneaking around as usual? There’s nothing to see here.
And my greetings to you. What are you doing with these priestesses?
I would tell you it is none of your business, scout, but since you asked so nicely: we are providing a public service. Removing the weak and wounded so they do not burden the living.
Actually, you could be useful. You see, a few lightweavers escaped, and we need them back to complete the… service.
So it is what I thought. Let them go! [Attack]
The service, right. Indeed, not our business, so we will be on our way. [Leave]
If they escaped, are they really that weak? We could help you, but do mind that we are not a charity.
No need to worry about that. Even the weak have enough life force to share among us.
Agreed then.
Perfect, I was worried you’d be gone. Sorry, sunshine, but you are coming with us.
What? Where — No! No you cannot! You wicked beast! Better that we die fighting you!
And what brought you back, The Agent of Alvar?
No need for such a grudge: your sisters are safe.
In fact, they are with me.
…Oh.
This minotaur is escorting us to the abbey! Without him, we would have suffered a terrible fate.
Our deepest gratitude to you, warriors of Alvar. We will immediately go tending to our sisters’ wounds.
May the Sun shine upon all your endeavors.
[Attack and Defense increased by 2]
They are? And why would I believe you?
We found the group of necromancers you were talking about. By what they told me, they were doing a “public service,” to help the living get rid of the weak and wounded.
If I wanted to lie to you, I would make a more intricate lie.
I believe you. Well. Our gratitude, then. May the light of Chalice show you the right way in the future.
[Attack and Defense increased by 2]
Oh, look who is back. Have your ideals become loftier?
Actually, they have, yes.
No, not really. [Leave]
Huh. I see. Well, better late than never. We are coming with you, in case you change your ideals again…
[The Sun Heralds joined your army]
No need to have such a grudge against me. You should understand better than anyone that — sometimes — our duty prevails.
Fine. Leave, then, agent of Alvar.
I don’t think they will dare to come back here soon.
The lightweavers couldn’t run too far. Probably you can catch them somewhere on the way to that precious abbey of theirs.
Oh, I see you are back — perfect, perfect! Just on time!
[Chained Priestesses removed from your inventory]

The necromancers’ ritual twists the light of the lightweavers into a power the Temple itself would deem unholy. The sight is far from pleasant, yet amidst it all, you feel a strange warmth seeping into you — an unnatural surge of life, both empowering and unsettling.
[Spell Power and Knowledge increased by 4]
The life force of others is pulsating within me, turning into real power. And I will definitely find a use for it.
We applaud you making the right choice. Power over weakness.
It seems empty… I wonder where the other sisters are…
[Injured Priestesses removed from your inventory]
I believe our duty is done here.
Thank you, warriors! Without you, we would have been doomed!
Please let us share our modest knowledge with you!
[“Haste” added to the Spellbook]
May the light of the Chalice guide your path!
May your souls never see the darkness!
May you —
Thank you! I think we’ve got the idea! Farewell!
Please… save them…
Look, sisters: the brave warriors of Alvar are back!
Sun Everlasting shall guide your path!
Let —
Thank you, and goodbye!
Are you alright?
By the Chalice’s mercy, yes. I wish my sisters were here, though…
Please, take us back to the Abbey.
[Received Injured Priestesses]
By Chalice’s grace! You are safe, sisters! I feared I’d never see you again in this world!
Thank you for saving us!
[Received Injured Priestesses]
And our deepest gratitude to you, warriors of Alvar. May the Sun shine upon all your endeavors!
[Attack and Defense increased by 2]
Now we’ll have to tend to our sisters’ wounds. Please help us get to our Abbey safely.
[Sun Heralds left your army]
Nothing personal. I made a deal, and you’re the payment. Chain them!
Only a creature that sees no light could do something like this!
Well… We don’t get much sun in the dungeons, so I suppose you are right.
[Received Chained Priestesses]
Huh? Have you seen this? Can’t pick it up — my hand goes straight through it!
Horned one! Heeey! Over here!
You wanted that? That’s ours, you know!
Eh, not quite. It is just an illusion! But we do have the genuine thing, too!
You want it?
Well… I don’t not‑want it.
Come ooon! You need to speak with more confidence!
Yeah! Otherwise, you ain’t ever getting the things you want in this life!
Cough. I want the artifact!
That’s more like it!
Also, we’re not giving it to you.
Well, not for free, anyway.
You are a hero, right? You do quests and stuff? So why don’t you do something for us and you get a reward!
Sigh. I guess. What is it that you two want?
Take this scroll.
Huh?
You heard me! Take this scroll! It contains a powerful spell! You need to release its spell or the world will be doomed!
DOOOMED!!
But mind that these scrolls are tEmPoRaRy. You release the spell, and then woooshhhh! The scroll goes away! So… take it!
Well, I guess the artifact is worth it.
Huh? Who wants a temporary scroll? I’ll pass, then.
Awesome! That’s the spirit!
You will help us out! And the whole world!
[You gained the Weakening Ray scroll!]
Come back when you cast the spell!
Booo!
Come on, sis. Let’s go. This one is no fun.
Forget about the item, then, you oaf!
Hey, you are still walking around with that super dangerous scroll we gave you!
We can sense it on you! Better use it soon before it is too late!
You want the item, right?
Yeah, yeah. Just give me some more time.
No. I’m done being your errand boy.
Whaaaat? But what about our fun?!
Come on, he’s seen through us. Lets just leave this spoilsport be.
Meanie! Guess you aren’t worthy of the item after all!
Oh! You released the spell, didn’t you? How fast!
Did you like it…?
Well, I saved the world didn’t I?
Yeah, sure thing! Did you have fun, though?
I’m sure he did! Our big, horned savior! However, a good hero’s work is never done so easily!
Huh? Oh, right! Yea, there is more to do! There is another scroll!
You must use it too if you want to reeaally save the world and get your rightful reward!
Again? Ugh, alright.
No. I’m done being your errand boy.
Who knew there were so many dangerous, doom‑invoking scrolls around?!
Yeah, right?! So off you go, and don’t come back until you release the spell! We are counting on you!
[You gained the Shade Cloak scroll!]
…and your reward will be waiting for you!
Whaaaat? But what about our fun?!
Come on, he’s seen through us. Lets just leave this spoilsport be.
Meanie! Guess you aren’t worthy of the item after all!
The hero returns! Such triumph! Such glory!
Wooooow!
Now that you used the second scroll, It’s almoooost enough to save the world.
You just need to do one more! To save it For Good!
Fine. But there’d better not be any more after this one!
Enough of this! Keep your damn trinket!
It’s the last one!
Yeah, we promise! Pixies’ word!
[You gained the Cave In scroll!]
Booooo angry minotaur!
Booo, how stereotypical!
The pixies blow raspberries and fly away.
You are back, and no scroll on you!
Give me my reward already.
Well… we would love to…
But there is another scroll!
What?! You said it was the last one!
Ah yeah… well, we lied! But this one is!
Pinky swear!
…fine.
Enough of this! Keep your damn trinket!
Yay! Take it!
[You gained the Spatial Snare scroll!]
Ahaha!
Booooo angry minotaur!
Booo, how stereotypical!
The pixies blow raspberries and fly away.
Hey, you are still walking around with that super dangerous scroll we gave you!
Better use it soon before it is too late!
You want the item, right?
Yeah, yeah. Just give me some more time.
Enough of this! Keep your damn trinket!
Aw, don’t get angry!
We had lots of fun! Shame you don’t feel like playing anymore.
You also helped us get rid of these damn scrolls cluttering up our home! So for that, you are certainly a great hero in our book!
Take the artifact. You earned it!
And remember to have fun out there!
Welcome back, scroll boy!
Want another?
Give it. They are actually quite useful.
Enough of this! Keep your damn trinket!
Wha‑at? W‑we actually don’t have any more!
Yeah, we just needed somebody to get rid of all the junk scrolls we had lying around cluttering up our homes!
Thanks for helping out with that, by the way! Always wanted to be a quest‑giver!
Take the item. You earned it!
And since you ACTUALLY used the scrolls!
Yeah! Like PROPERLY!
We will teach you how to use them fOrEvEr!
[“Weakening Ray,” “Shade Cloak,” “Cave In,” and “Spatial Snare” added to the Spellbook]
Aw, don’t get angry!
We had lots of fun! Shame you don’t feel like playing anymore.
You also helped us get rid of these damn scrolls cluttering up our home! So for that, you are certainly a great hero in our book!
Take the artifact. You earned it!
And remember to have fun out there!
Didn’t take long. Now to get my reward.
Done! Now I just need to get back.
This has to be the last one, right…?
Well… at least I think I’m getting the hang of this!
Not going to be stuck running around with some scroll. At least I can get some valuable resources.
Well, if they are going to keep letting me do this…
Well… more dust for me, I guess.
Imagine I actually ran around casting all those. Like I have nothing better to do!
Would have never noticed you there if I didn’t know what to look for. Alvar has its eyes everywhere, huh?
Information is our lifeblood. Especially now, with another war brewing between the Templars and the necromancers.
Hardly a war yet. Just a slightly exaggerated disagreement.
You never know when things might escalate. That said, you seem to be well on your way to resolving this situation. I see no reason to linger.
There’s a hidden pathway around here — I’ll leave it open for you. Farewell.
You never know when things might escalate.
There’s a hidden pathway around here — you can use it. I’ll stick around for now.
You never know when things might escalate. That said, you seem to be well on your way to resolving this situation. I see no reason to linger.
There’s a hidden pathway around here — I’ll leave it open for you. Farewell.
Wait! I have a request.
There’s a certain document in Sunspire — a report from a scout who served one Knight Valentina, a tax collector. Could you steal it for me?
Of course.
…“Of course”? That’s it? No payment? No hesitation?
You’re the Triumvirate’s free agent. Your will is the will of the Triumvirate.
I’ll see you in Alvar.
Staying on the lookout. Nothing seems out of order.
I have a request.
There’s a certain document in Sunspire — a report from a scout who served one Knight Valentina, a tax collector. Could you steal it for me?
Of course.
…“Of course”? That’s it? No payment? No hesitation?
You’re the Triumvirate’s free agent. Your will is the will of the Triumvirate.
I’ll see you in Alvar.
You there. You’re from Alvar, aren’t you?
You could say that. We’ve come to assist the governor of Sunspire while carrying out our own mission.
To some extent. We were sent to investigate strange reports from our envoys, many of whom have gone missing.
It depends on what you want from us.
In that case, I’ll be blunt.
Governor Allistair is a good man — in peacetime. He cares about his people, which is exactly why we’re under attack.
The governor refuses to levy troops. Says untrained peasants are no match for real soldiers. So Sun’s Watch is barely protected — no surprise someone took advantage of that.
The governor is right. Farmhands are meant for fields of wheat, not the battlefield.
The city’s starved for defenders — you’ll need all the help you can get.
Sound logic… in peacetime. But right now, we cannot afford such luxuries.
Glad we see eye to eye.
We’ve made an arrangement with your embassy, granting you certain authority within our borders to deal with Allistair’s… idealism.
Scout the land. Visit the villages. Convince them to join the fight. We’ll have more troops if you rally them. As long as it’s seen as voluntary, there won’t be any scandal.
If you help us, I’ll send letters ahead, easing your passage. Consider it a gesture of gratitude.
Sounds sensible. We’ll do it.
Destroying your food supply won’t help in the long run. We’ll handle the attackers without it.
Recruiting villagers is cruel. We’ll deal with the attackers without this.
Hey, there.
Aah! What’s this now? Another one o’ them nondead monsters?
Undead. And no, I’m not one of them. Calm down. We serve the Alvar Dominion, not the Guild.
Ain’t now way ye be from Alvar! Ain’t no one from there come this far!
We are here to help with the undead threat —
Don’t care what ye say! Ye’re big and scary like ’em!
This is getting out of hand.
Now that the dust has settled, who among you is head of the village?
Have mercy, sire. We’re simple folk. These fields’re all we know.
I’m not here to slay you, but to help you.
The news may have not reached you yet, but the undead are coming this way. Do nothing, and your village will be consumed by the horde.
However, the general has a proposition: those of you capable of fighting — armed with blades or crossbows — are welcome to join his army and defend the helpless. Follow us, and we’ll lead you to him.
You heard the man? Alright, just follow him and protect us. You have my blessing.
Arina’s sweet bosom, a proper mino’aur!
A change for once. Why are you wasting time in the fields when your lands are overrun by the undead?
I’d love t’fight, but… me fa’her is sick an’ me sister is jus’ a wee lass. I’m th’ only sturdy pair o’ mitts ’ere.
I’m sure the necromancers will appreciate your reasoning as they raise your father’s and sister’s dead bodies.
I know how important family is. Fine. We’ll try to look for something that could heal your pa.
I understand how much family means. We’ve got something that can heal your pa. Will you enlist if I give it to him? [Give Lifeblood Fae artifact]
…You’re right. I wasn’t thinkin’ straight.
I’ll join now. Best get to fightin’.
…You’d do that? Can’t say I expected such kindness… Thank ye, good people. Thank ye.
Don’t thank us yet. We haven’t found anything but trouble. But I’ll try.
You kiddin’? Of course I will! Never thought I’d see such kindness…
Thank ye! Thank ye! I’ll get to the general right away!
Any news on that heal’n item?
Still on it.
I’ve changed my mind. You should be willing to protect your home without any handouts from an outsider.
…I see. Yer right, I s’pose. But ya didn’t need to give me false hope.
I’ll remember this — never trust outsiders.
Here, take this. Give it to your father.
[Lifeblood Fae removed from your inventory]
Thank ya. Don’t rightly know what to say.
[Received 10 Wood and 10 Ore]
No one’s ever gone through so much for me… Much less an outsider. Maybe folks from Alvar really are better than us.
More metropolitan, that’s for sure.
Perhaps we are.
One can be kind to people regardless of what they look like, y’know.
Oi, ye the new recruiter?
How did you know?
Gossip, o’ course.
Huh.
This be a village. Ain’t no fun but swappin’ tales ’round the fire.
What’s the juiciest recent gossip?
Ready to be recruited, then?
Oh, you didn’t hear? Old Jareth’s been mumblin’ ’bout seein’ a shadow out by the woods. Tall’s a tree, no shape, jus’ a dark blur. Swears he saw it with his own eyes, he did.
Ain’t nobody took him seriously, but now some others been talkin’ ’bout it too. Could be the undead muckin’ around, or something else. Folk ’round here are gettin’ nervous.
A strange shadow, huh?
I’ll bet it’s the necromancers raising the dead to move about in the dark and trick your minds. Some would say it’s below the Guild’s dignity, but I wouldn’t be surprised if I turned out to be right.
Tall as a tree, you say? If something is moving in the woods, I’d blame the woods themselves. The Murmurwoods, to be precise. But I don’t believe they intend to harm you.
Let me tell you a secret: It’s probably Alvar’s spies moving around. But don’t tell anyone.
Aye, but who knows? Folks ’round here don’t take kindly to the unknown. It stirs fear.
Ready to be recruited, then?
Yeah. Not like there’s anyone left here anymore.
That should be it. These people won’t ever realize, but we likely saved their lives.
Let’s hope they won’t forget Alvar’s help.
Hm… I cannot approach it.
You sense an enigmatic presence lingering in the air. Though its nature eludes you, an instinct deep within urges absolute stillness, as if only in perfect quiet can you claim the artifact.
You sense a subtle shift in the air.
The presence has grown stronger.
The presence has faded.
This portal cannot be entered. It seems to work only as the exit point.
So, the rogue medusae are responsible.
Hardly a surprise; they’ve stirred trouble before.
That makes this an internal matter. We must act before the Templars catch wind of it and send their envoys prying into our affairs.
These medusae have always been a fickle people — clinging to their fractured ways instead of embracing the strength of unity.
The so‑called “Wild Ones,” yes. They feel unheard, unseen by us.
Nonsense. They were summoned to serve. This is precisely why we never made a Quadrumvirate! The Wild Ones simply prefer to remain invisible — along with their unsavory ways and rituals.
Amelchia, a ruler must temper judgement with wisdom…
And I must temper my patience before you descend into this long‑solved and tiresome debate.
Our task is clear — either rescue Valentina or extract answers from those who took her.
Venturing into the deepest caves isn’t a task for one. Gunnar has already put in a great effort. We should send someone with him.
On this one, I agree. Let’s send that elemental whelp with him. The depths are unpredictable; better to have something equally unpredictable on our side.
While I don’t disagree, my best people are tied up dealing with those disturbances in the Murmurwoods.
And before you ask — my worst have simply vanished into thin air. Unless you want a halfwit for the task, we’ll need to wait until I have someone suitable.
Now, can we move on to the next matter, or must I witness yet another battle of ideals?
We shall proceed. Yet I cannot help but ponder — had we chosen to side with the necromancers instead, would we have learned more?..
I reckon that concludes our mission here. The only thing left is to report back to Alvar.

So, you want to know more about this “inextinguishable” fire? Hardly my area of expertise; I am more a warrior than a scholar. However, my commander, Lord Galthran, should be knowledgeable enough to assist you… if you help us in return.
You see, the Templars are meddling in our affairs again. Can we rely on you to show them the way out? They seem to be lost.

So, the Temple is meddling in your affairs, and you think it’s wise of Alvar to join forces with the Necromancers Guild and interfere with the Dusk Treaty in response? Sounds like the beginning of a much larger war to me.

Oh, spare me the pretense — you made your decision long before coming here. And who said anything about the Guild? Lord Galthran does not consider himself part of that… group. If he doesn’t, why should I? Secondly —
Not part of the Guild? I thought all necromancers belonged to it.
Secondly?
No, not all of us. Not Lord Galthran and his followers, at least.
And that’s why you’ve decided to attack the Temple?
And that’s why the Templars deemed it lawful to attack you?

Refusing to follow the decrees of dusty old scholars whose greatest power is to bore you to death doesn’t make us savages. Those sunlickers have been sending incursions into our lands for weeks now, stirring unrest and disturbing the dead. We are merely returning the favor.

As to their motives, I am as clueless as you are. One day, they simply began the threats, demanding we move away from their border — not that we’ve ever been near it. Only their sunshine‑and‑rainbows god knows what they truly want from us.

Secondly, we have heard whispers of a scout recruiting soldiers. On our land. Hardly usual behavior for the Temple’s subjects. She was muttering some nonsense similar to yours: unstoppable fire, someone abducted, and the like. So… assist us, and we will not hinder your little search party trampling our grass.
And you’re only telling me now, huh? Fine, we will help you, but don’t expect me to enjoy it.
Hmmm. She might just be mad, but it’s still worth investigating. You can count on us.
Who goes there?! In Lord Edgar’s name, reveal yourself!
Gunnar, son of Gunnar. My scouting group came here on the behalf of the Triumvirate and at the necromancers’ request. Tell me, why did you ransack the villages in their lands?
Why these incursions? I thought the Dusk Treaty was still in force.
A shame. I had hoped for diplomacy. [Attack]
So, they got to you, too. Don’t trust their lies — their words are poison, and they trample all over the Dusk Treaty. Turn back now, or face the Sun’s wrath.
They see it differently. To them, you struck first. Surely, this can be resolved without bloodshed?
I seek only passage and answers. Take me to your lord.
I see. Then I’ll find my answers after I get past you.
Lord Edgar commands it — the darklings must fall. Any who stand with them are enemies of the Chalice. Stand aside, minotaur.
Do all Templars talk in orders? I’ve had better conversations with statues. Sigh.
Either way, we will have to learn of this Lord Edgar and his intentions.
Enough talk! Show me if your skill matches your faith!
They’ve broken through the previous line of defense! Summon reinforcements! Lord Edgar’s plan must not fail!
Stop this madness! None of this bloodshed is necessary!
You sealed your fate when you sided with deaders!
All this talk, yet and I still don’t know why you’re here.
Have the lightheads truly lost their minds?
Archers — aim!
No time to waste — in the name of the Triumvirate!
This should put an end to the Templar activity here. That lord… Was Galthran his name? He will welcome some respite.
I hope this does not delay my task — finding the scout now will be tricky.
That armor — wait. Is this the scout that the necromancer mentioned? I think it is.
And of course, she is dead. Aren’t we lucky?
Thankfully, not irreversible. Her soul hasn’t yet vacated her mortal coil.
And you are…?
Maybe we actually are lucky! You are a boneseeker, aren’t you? And this means she has yet to undergo the rites.
How did she die?
My name’s Klastor, but that is none of your concern. I’m not a combatant, if that’s what you’re wondering; and neither did I kill her.
Always a pleasure to be praised to the degree we deserve. And no, this is not what I am here for.
Not by my hand, if that’s what you’re intending.
There’s a conflict going on. It tends to kill people.
I take it you had some business with the lady?
Correct. She might know something about the matter we are trying to investigate.
Aha, well, this is none of my concern.
But what I can see is that you need her. I can help you — for a price of course — so everyone is going to be content! Well, except her, probably. But isn’t it great, anyway?
And why do you say she won’t be content?
Well… Humans that serve the Temple are never happy to receive the gift of undeath. Something about the soul joining the sun or similar nonsense. Good thing we ain’t asking, right?
I need to hear what she saw, so… What payment do you want?
The beliefs of the Temple may not be to my taste, but it doesn’t feel fair to disturb a dead person with something they wouldn’t enjoy while still alive.
I’ve heard there’s an artifact called Shard of Serenity hidden somewhere around. Bring it to me, and you’ll get to have a word with the girl.
All right. We’ll be back with the artifact. Doesn’t really matter. We’ll bring it.
Can’t I just pay enough for you to hire another courier?
That would be very generous of you, but I am not resurrecting the girl for gold. Only for the artifact. Now, start searching, or accept that she is dead for good.
It can’t ever be easy, can it?
And somehow you are allied with the necromancers? You know that this eternal‑soul concept, return to the sun or become a bush and blah‑blah‑blah, is not something we care about?

You die, and then you rot. Or! You can master the intricate art of necromancy and become immortal. Truly immortal, and not those nonsense fairytales. And those who can’t or won’t do that receive the service and are repurposed in the fissures of Shadowspire, as it has always been.

However, if you also prefer the children’s stories and believe that they are worth rejecting my offer… The scout was probably coming from Sunspire, and she must have left a report there — all scouts do. Go seek it, if you have nothing better to do.
Papers in the human city, huh? I think I know someone who can steal them for me.
Papers in the human city, huh? Then I’ll just find someone who can steal them for me.
Fine, fine, you are right. The scout’s life and death are none of my concern. We’re resurrecting her. What payment do you want?
Hah! Well, fortunate for you, I planned to stick around for a while. Let me know when you change your mind.
I’ll only ask for one artifact for my services!
Back so soon?
As I said, bring me the Shard of Serenity — and interrogate the scout all you like. This aimless nonsense is a waste of time.
Fine, I’ll get it for you.
Calm down, here is your artifact. [Give Shard of Serenity]
I’m still considering my options.
Well, that’s some good news, isn’t it? Now we can proceed!
Did you bring the Shard of Serenity?
Not yet.
Then what are you doing here? Keep searching.
Calm down, here is your artifact.
[The Shard of Serenity artifact removed from your inventory]
Well, that’s some good news, isn’t it? Now we can proceed!
We don’t have to use your services after all.
Pah. Not going to cry about it; I still have her body. Her bones will eventually benefit someone else, and not you.
At least my hands are clean.
I strongly doubt she would agree to undergo the rituals of Shadowspire! Seize him! [Attack]
Chalice, please accept this servant’s soul. Chalice, please accept this servant’s soul. Chalice —
Wait — Where am I? Who are you?
My name is Gunnar. Unfortunately, you died, but I had to disturb you. I need to ask a few questions about your last —
Disturb me?! You mean… what have you done?! I — I was so close! I could feel the Sun! Why did you drag me back? Why?!
Curse you, defiler! May the Sun turn its light away from your spirit! Die!!!
I felt the Chalice… the Sun’s warm, all‑consuming embrace… Why did you have to chain me to the wretched cage of endless existence?
I’m sorry. I regret what I’ve done.
I need you to tell me about your last mission. This goes beyond your personal fate — the entire destiny of Jadame might be in the balance.
Save your regrets! They mean nothing to me!
I understand, but I still need you to tell me about your last mission. The very destiny of Jadame could be at stake.
Is that so…?
Why hasn’t Valentina reached the village of Garner Hills?
Valentina… I served in her army. We were tasked with collecting taxes from the farmers, but we were ambushed. The medusae struck from every direction. The fire… it wouldn’t stop burning.
The fire that never goes out. I’ve seen it. Does it have something to do with the medusae?
They captured her… they captured Valentina…
Hm. Does not seem like she knows anything else.
More ominous mysteries. Guess we’ll be paying the Wild Ones a visit. Even if the abduction does not have anything to do with the fire, either Valentina or her captors may know something about it.
Woe… Woe to me…
You again? Don’t you have better places to be?
A vampire unaffiliated with the Guild! Now that is a rarity.

Indeed. But more importantly, he could prove a valuable asset if we secure his allegiance. The Guild’s influence grows unchecked, and having an independent necromancer in our fold… That is an advantage worth seizing.
And you are not concerned that his loyalty might be just as fleeting with us?
The risk is worth it. Amelchia is right — the Guild’s dominance may one day challenge our own. We need someone who knows their methods.
And let’s be honest, Caspair: you’d relish adding another agent to your web.
You said it, not me.
Then it is decided. We will forge an alliance, ensuring it is lucrative enough that even the vampire finds loyalty preferable.
And while we have his ear, let’s at least ask about the unquenchable flames. One can never predict the depths of a mage’s knowledge.
I’m glad you two are so excited about the vampire, but I need to remind you: Gunnar has already done much, but he cannot venture into Galthran’s domain alone. We should send someone with him.
On this one, I agree. Let’s send that elemental whelp with him. The depths are unpredictable; better to have something equally unpredictable on our side.
While I don’t disagree, my best people are tied up dealing with those disturbances in the Murmurwoods.
And before you ask — my worst have simply vanished into thin air. Unless you want a halfwit for the task, we’ll need to wait until I have someone suitable.
Agreed, then. Now, can we move on, or would you both prefer to keep celebrating our newfound vampire?
We shall proceed. Yet I cannot help but ponder — had we chosen to side with the Templars instead, would we have learned more?..
And here we are — back in the Murmurwoods.
If luck is on our side, we won’t have to get tangled in the local mess and waste our people on it.
Not entangled…? Oh, that would be wondrous!
The root fire, it stirs, and we are closer to it! But…
Of course there’s a “but.” There is always a “but” in these damned woods. Out with it.
There is no path, no road‑walk that leads there. None that we can distinguish…
But, there — the energy is still leaking out! It’s boiling, and the steam… is whispering through the cracks!
Sigh. Understanding you is trickier than I’d hoped.
Fine. How about this: We scout the area, figure out what we’re dealing with, and get out as soon as possible. I’d rather not linger here longer than necessary.
Why not? Are minotaurs not friends with trees? We were told you build a lot! Trees are useful to build things.
First, I’m a scout, not an architect. Second, it’s not about trees. It’s about these trees. They… whisper. It’s sending shivers down my spine.
So you take the lead this time, and we’ll all just follow you. You are more attuned to this place, and I’m already stuck relying on your elemental senses anyway.
We get to lead…? Then… let’s not waste time…! To adventure!
Can’t you just re‑open this portal with your elemental powers?
We cannot. We are disappointed in ourselves, too…
Sigh. Then we will have to find that old man from your vision.
Elemental one! I blocked the way out of need. Search for me in the South!
I sensed your arrival, but I did not expect you to find me so quickly.
Welcome, wayfarers. My name is Ignatius — though most simply call me the Old Pilgrim. What brought such an unusual party to the depths of the Murmurwoods?
Greetings, silver‑haired man! Strange, fiery energy stirs from within your woods. We cannot explain it, but we distinguish it clearly from afar! The Triumvirate has sent us to uncover its source.
Fiery energy? Hah, you have no idea how close to the truth you are, elemental. Still… I cannot let you through so easily.
And why not?

I do not trust you. You are strangers to the Murmurwoods. You march in with an army, and two others here already kill each other in a nonsense quarrel for this land. I cannot afford to watch over you as well.
If you wish to pass, you must first end the conflict raging in this domain. I care not how — it must stop. The forest demands it. The Templars and the wood‑dwellers will not heed my words.
Aren’t you a druid? Shouldn’t the locals at least listen to you?
Aren’t you a former Templar? Why would the knights disregard you?
I am a druid, yes. But that grants me no command over the dwellers. Alvar may be the land of free people, but the Murmurwoods are the land of free will.
“Former” is the key word. To the Church, I am heretic at best — criminal at worst.
And they are not blameless in this war, either.
You may try diplomacy, but I fear steel is the only tongue left in this conflict. While I hold back the energy beyond the portals, the rest falls to you.
That sounds like a whole pile of “not our problem.” If you are the one barring our way, why don’t we just deal with you instead?
Ha‑ha! You are mighty, but young, my friend. I may look old, but I know how to fight. When I served the Church, my name was not spoken lightly. And here, the forest fights at my side.
Your odds of defeating me are… slim.
It appears so, druid.
You think too highly of yourself. In the names of the Forefathers! Secure them! [Attack]
We’ll stop the fighting, and then you let us through.
We have an agreement, then. I await your return.
Welcome, wayfarers. My name is Ignatius — though most simply call me the Old Pilgrim. What brought such an unusual party to the depths of the Murmurwoods?
Greetings, silver‑haired man! Strange, fiery energy stirs from within your woods. We cannot explain it, but we distinguish it clearly from afar! The Triumvirate has sent us to uncover its source.
Fiery energy? Hah, you have no idea how close to the truth you are, elemental. But, seeing as you’ve managed to end the conflict that has been raging in this realm, you might soon find out.
It is unfortunate. Neither the fey nor the Templars were willing to listen to me, and the forest wanted an end to the fighting.
And now that it has ended, I can focus on making sure that your interaction with the flaming power ahead remains contained.
Go to the portals. Just… try to steel yourself for what you will find beyond.
A mystery! Exciting!
Well? Have you made any progress on bringing peace back to these woods?
Well…
