If you are still thinking about fighting me, then you are thinking about meeting your end.
Alright, alright. We will return once the conflict is finished.
Let’s see if there is any truth to your threats. Troops, prepare for battle! [Attack]
Should’ve just surrendered.
Never! This is our land. We will never yield — to blind fanatics of the Temple nor to mindless servants of Alvar!
Back to the roots… they are gone…
Our insides are bawling with gloom.
This is how wars are, Lafiur. Little we can do about it. I am sorry to say it, but not picking a side is a privilege that not everyone can afford.
The Temple of the Sun won’t forget this! Alvar defies our lawful right to these lands!
If Caspair sends me to the Frozen Vast after this, it’s your fault, Lafiur.
Now, we should focus on why we initially came here and figure out how we will get to the energy source.
You have done well. I shall open the portals once more and watch over you as you venture inside.
Just… steel yourself for what lies beyond.
It’s… impossible…
I told you, you think too highly of yourself.
The paths are opening…! We suppose this was one way of handling the situation…?
Hello… Can you hear me?
I heard it… I heard the call… But I can never reach it…!
New voice…? Hah… hahaha… Yes, I can hear you…!
Did you come to end my agony…?
Be careful. He has been trapped like this for too long. Even I cannot say if there is anything left of him beyond the suffering.
We came to understand what is happening here. We followed the intuition of my friend…
Hello! You seem to be somewhat burning too, like us… Like Fiur.
…and we found you. What happened?
Ahaha… ha…
I came to the forest… like you. Sent here with the others. By order of the Temple.
We were just collecting some wood. I went for a stroll at night…
And then… I heard a voice.
A voice?
A chorus.
It rang inside me, through my bones, my soul. And then my heart… It burned.
The voice came from that scarlet star, as it hung in the sky. It called me. It offered me everything I ever wanted.
And I still reach for it… But the roots…!
The roots! How did you become… a plant part?
The forest… It took hold of me, too. I think… I think it’s trying to heal me… to protect me from the call of the Star.
But I don’t want it! I want to join that Chorus! The Chorus pulls me up to the sky and the roots… they pull me down to the ground! They tear at me, pull me apart!
One calls me forward, into the transformation… The other drags me back, to everything I once was.
I cannot escape either. I cannot bear it…
We can discern two forces within him… One is the Murmurwoods, the other… We… don’t know it?
No… we don’t. But I do. I know that I know it from before. But we don’t remember.
Can we do anything for you?..
End it. Kill me.
No. I cannot grant you the gift of mercy.
All right. I suppose you deserve to be free.
Something like you doesn’t just happen. Maybe you can still serve a purpose.
T‑that does not make it any better…
Thank you. Sweet release…
Hammer my horns… Is that a human?
We wish we knew what it was… But we do not know what it is.
I think he is still alive…
Your entangling must stop! Please, turn your warriors around. We do not wish you harm!
An elemental creature, and still you choose to rub shoulders with Templars? That’s despicable treason! We move as one! Fey, formations!
What else a filthy fey would say! Of course you would side with them! For the love of the Chalice! Charge!
Such a waste of precious lives.
Knights guarding a mere construction site?
These woods are more perilous than they appear…
At times, it feels as though something watches us from the shadows. Sometimes, even the trees seem to have eyes.
Don’t tell me you’re frightened.
I am a brave man — but even courage has its limits. Just this week, I saw creatures I scarce believed real. Enormous birds wreathed in flame, brighter than the midday Sun.
Who knows what other wonders — or horrors — lurk within this forest?
Hm. Would you happen to know where we might find these fiery birds?
Seeking danger, are you? I saw them off to the north… but don’t blame me if you end up roasted like a festival ox.
The buildings! They are all on fire! What have you done?!

What!? Where’d he go? Hmph. Guess he made his choice.
Job’s done. The beast is slain.
raspy sigh
Thank you.
Now I will leave. There’s much I need to reflect on…
You return.
We brought you a friend. See, there was no monster to be afraid of.
[The monster? stops following you]
It… reminds me. My father was a lord… He built kennels for his dogs. I used to play with them when I was young.
Then maybe you can give this one a home?
Woof!
I… Thank you.
Dangerous looking beast.
But like most feral things, it’s more afraid of us than we are of it.
Beasts are there to be slain. [Attack]
I have other things to do for now. [Leave]
Grrrr!
So, this is the monster? It looks more scared of us than we are of it.
Sorry, but you didn’t have to terrorize this forest. [Attack]
Easy… Easy…?
Grrrr!
It’s not attacking. I wonder… What would an undead dog want?
Maybe we can try to toss a stick? [Give 1 Wood]
Maybe we can give it some blood? [Lose 1 Defense for a day]
Let’s think a bit more. [Leave]
Woof!
Well, I’ll be… I think I know somebody who would like to meet you. Someone who is pretty lonely.
Ruff?
[The monster? follows you]
BARK! BARK!
Damn it! The sight of blood caused it to fly into a frenzy!
Clover and I will always remember your help.
Thank you once again. This creature was much like me. Lost.
I will make sure that it does not attack others again.
So you really had nothing to return to?
No… But now, I can continue to exist for somebody.
Woof!
[You gained the Sixth Finger artifact!]
[You gained the Monster Head artifact!]
Winter is coming…
Winter? Bah! With all those fire insects buzzing about, it might as well be summer all year!
grumbles Don’t mind that sourpuss. Winter is always coming!
Apparently, Alvar is forming a coalition. What are the odds we’ll be part of it?
The undead in a coalition? Preposterous! That’s never happened!
Perhaps it ought to. Who says we can’t be on the right side for once? I’m rather tired of only consorting with those mad liches.
Their humor is as dead as their armies!
Things were so much better in the old days…
Here you go again.
Am I mistaken? Wasn’t the grass grimier, the troglodytes more plentiful, and the treasure chests overflowing with gold?
And heroes actually took the time to learn something!
Now you’re just spouting nonsense. All these adventurers have ever learned to do is how to loot the crypts and incite the peasantry against us!
What has happened to digging ground?
Jadame simply doesn’t hold anything of value just below the surface. And as long as those blasted Regnan pirates don’t show up, it should remain that way.
Ever consider retiring?
Every single day. I’d fancy moving to Enroth — I hear the sun’s dimmer there.
Just you wait; I’ve heard Thant might even dim the sun entirely. Then we’ll be able to truly live it up!
Right you are! That’ll be the day pigs learn to fly!
Is there anything left in that manor behind us?
Nothing at all — only our heartwood coffins.
Splendid. Then no one will be tempted to rummage through our remains.
[Continue listening]
[Leave]
[Attack]
There’s nothing here.
Nothing but a few… wooden boxes?
They’re called coffins.
Huh? But they don’t cough…
…I’ll explain on the way. Let’s move.
Well, well. Care to introduce yourself properly?
Hmph. You do enjoy your formalities, don’t you?
Very well — Galthran, Lord of Grimstone Ridge. Renowned vampire. Death knight.
And, from our information, a death knight unaffiliated with the Guild. Correct?
Quite so. I refuse to have my ambitions stifled by a bureaucrat like Thant!
Then you might find Alvar’s offer quite… promising.
From my reports, you strike me as a talented general.
Oh, flattery is always welcome, but if you think a few compliments will win my loyalty, you’re mistaken. What exactly is in this for me?
Weren’t we the ones helping you escape Thant’s forces? That was our part of the deal.
Bah! I don’t rely on favors. I adapt. With or without your intervention, I would not have remained trapped for long.
You will have full access to Alvar’s resources and its extensive network of spies.
Aha! Now that’s worthy of my attention. Continue.

Balthazar, allow me. Lord Galthran, let me remind you — Jadame is about to be consumed by a force greater than you can handle alone. You do not want to join the Guild. And if you refuse to side with us…
…then your only option is to sprout chitin wings and start breathing fire. Hardly a fitting future for a lord, wouldn’t you say?
I… huh… had not considered matters under this specific angle. My apologies — I’m listening. What comes next?
Is the kidnapping of a knight a… common occurrence?
No. Which makes it all the more concerning.
A maze, this place… Twisting and turning. Like a wisp lost in the wind.
And dangerous. Renegade medusae dwell here — those who refused to side with Alvar.
We’ll need to split up, cover more ground. But stay sharp.
Those hydras… They were strange. Too coordinated. Almost like they were all tethered to something.
Could there be any truth to the stories of chthonic powers buried deep beneath Jadame — ancient forces that once ruled these lands?
Hm. This wild assignment is starting to make me sound like a prophet of doom. I need a rest.
Something stirs beneath the ground… We feel it.
Threads, woven together. Bound. Anchored. They are roots — trees — linked, like the Mycelium.
Who?
Hydras!
…What are you saying, hydras are trees now?
More so than you.
You are not making any sense…
…Fine! Let me try to understand.
The hydras, they did seem… off. As if something was guiding them. As well as stronger than I remember.
Lafiur, what do you think? Could it be that whatever connects these hydras is also making them more powerful?
Roots strengthening the trees? Naturally. It feels very much like that.
Then we’d better brace ourselves — the further we go, the stronger they’ll be.
Hssss! It is them.
Yes, this is me. Me and my army. Now, if you would kindly…
We shall ssspeak with you once, minotaur.
The one you seek is a tribute — taken by rightful force and prepared to be delivered. Let us deal with her as we see fit, and we will not harm you. We have no quarrel with you.
It is for a purpose far greater than you might underssstand. Surely you know the price and power of blood? Or have the Alvarians forgotten it entirely?
Blood is the force that binds this world. I respect that.
If the old blood magic is so powerful, why is it lost? I’ll tell you why: long‑term, social structure and progress beat any ancient rite.
At least you’re being prejudiced in a rather strange way.
Yessss. You underssstand.
You misjudge us, fool. But you’ll sssee. We will show you.
Trouble is, I really need to question your “tribute.” A lot of lives might be at stake. Let me pass.
You are not ssstrong enough to demand anything of us.
Now that I think about it, “lives at stake” wasn’t the best argument. Formations!
Valentina? From the Temple of the Sun?
…Who’s there? I need help. I’m hurt. Get me out of here!
Hang in there.
Thank the Chalice… and thank you. I owe you.
Words I never thought I’d hear from a tax collector.
Your sap is leaking.

My sap?… Are you from Alvar? The medusae took me while I was heading to Garner Hills. But from what I overheard… Alvar should be more worried than the Temple of the Sun. They were planning something — ah!
Apologies. I need to catch my breath and tend to these wounds… Oh, you are right, funny one — my sap is leaking!
We need to take you to Alvar anyway. You’ll report to the Triumvirate — and they’ll help with your injuries. Alright?
Please do. Like I said, your leaders have good reason to be concerned. And I’d be happy to explain why.
Of all the things that could find me, I didn’t expect… whatever you are.
Oh! You must be one of those spies! Gunnar told us all about you.
We’re supposed to let him know we found you.
Caspair mentioned Gunnar would be working with… someone different. Then I suppose I can share what I’ve learned. But keep this quiet.
Something is off within our own ranks. The renegade medusae have been spreading dangerous ideas — ones that match whispers I’ve uncovered back in the capital.
Are there traitors among us?
We face a war on two fronts. One from beyond… and one from within.
I need to return and report to the Triumvirate. They must hear this.
What are you two doing with that being?
Restoring rightful order.
Stand aside, mortal. Your oculi shall not be witness to sentinel activity.
Prepare to be removed forcefully.
Hammer my horns, it’s you again!
You are warned. Previous defiance has occurred. Interference is not permitted.
Prepare to be removed forcefully.
Hells. You again. What are you doing with that person? Are you finally going to explain yourselves, or will you just keep adding to the mystery?
We do not provide answers. Yet your presence may serve a greater purpose. Rather than altering history, we offer you a choice.
And there they go, vanishing once more.
So, any chance you can explain what’s going on here?
I have come seeking Valentina.
It seems we share a goal, then. But who were those beings who stopped you?
The Watchers. They determine if my mission is to proceed… or cease.
I’m not sure what the Watchers are, but now that they’ve gone… I have to decide myself if I can let your mission proceed.
Will you aid? Or obstruct my path?
What do you want with Valentina?
There is… knowledge she must receive. But it is hers alone to bear.
Knowledge? Let it be. I believe we could help each other.
Vague answers won’t work. Tell me more, or I’ll end you as a threat to my mission.
I might regret this… but I’m letting you go.
I don’t fully trust you, but I need your aid to reach Valentina. And you look like you could use mine.
I… have encountered more than mere opposition. The forces beyond the veil have set their sights upon me.
See? That’s why we should join forces. Once we reach Valentina, we can decide if we’re allies or enemies.
An alliance born of necessity… Fragile, like the strands of fate themselves. But my path is barren of companions.
Very well. I accept your offer.
[The Sentinel of Glory joined your army]
Words are fleeting, and truth is but a shard of the whole. If conflict is your will, then let it unfold.
Hmph. Still vague.
We are aligned in purpose — yet you choose to walk away?
I don’t trust you. Not enough to join forces. But I have no interest in fighting you either. I’ll take my chances and reach Valentina on my own. And most likely I’ll do it first.
Speed is not measured in steps alone. And Light does not race. It simply arrives.
We’ll see about that.
Halt! One of our siblings walks in your ranks!
This just keeps getting better…
We demand her return.
She said she came here seeking Valentina, which means our goals align. Why should I return her to you?
You do not grasp the gravity of the sin she seeks to commit. If she reaches Valentina, the purity of the Grand Experiment will be tainted. This cannot be allowed!
And what exactly is this “Grand Experiment” of yours?
We are not here to answer your queries. You will surrender our sister. In return, we offer you a blessing for your cooperation.
I’m not giving up allies for a promise of a blessing.
You know what? This is between you two, and I don’t want to be involved. So have her.
[Sentinel of Glory left your army]
Whatever this Grand Experiment is supposed to be, I think I’ll meddle with it.
Our gratitude upon you. May the blessing of Balance bring haste to your travels.
[Attack, Defense, Spell Power and Knowledge increased by 2]
I need your aid!
Not so fast in the end, huh?
This is not the time…
Sigh. Alright, alright, let me have a look. Forefathers! You are not looking good.
I believe we can’t just leave you here… Whoever you are. Who are you anyway?
I’m afraid we can’t afford to carry a wounded person around. Sorry.
A sentinel. We call ourselves sentinels.
A sentinel of what?
…An interesting inquiry. You mortals would call us guardians of righteousness… of glory.
Of glory, huh? Well, Sentinel of Glory, you’d better help me reach Valentina.
[The Sentinel of Glory joined your army]
…Cruelty.
Valentina. I have traversed great distances to find you.
Who are you, and what do you want with me? And why are you traveling with the Alvarian army?
We struck a deal, her and I. Both of us needed to find you, so we joined forces.
You must listen. You are not meant to be here. Not in this dungeon. Not in this world. You must come with me.
And where exactly is “with you”? You look like an angel, but not one of the Temple of the Sun.
Angel is what you mortals once called us. Before, we walked among you as allies. But that was before you came to this world.
You mean, before I was born? Also, once allies? So you’re a traitor to the Church, and you expect me to blindly follow you?
We are no traitors. The Church is.
Watch your tongue!
Truth does not yield to reverence. You are wounded, fragile. If my purpose was to bring you harm, would I not have struck already? Would I have opposed my own kind to reach you?
That… makes sense. But I still do not trust you. And I would sooner follow the armies of Alvar than your cryptic riddles.
I won’t force either of you to listen to the other. If the knight wants to leave, we leave.
You may not like it, but I trust the sentinel. She risked everything to find you.
Valentina, you walk toward a fate not meant for you.
So be it. Begone.
And? I have no interest in the squabbles of celestial beings.
You have more to do with them than you realize. Allow me to prove it.
Fine. Show me why the heavens concern themselves with me.

The sentinel produces a strange lantern, its form shifting unnaturally as if it were from a bygone era. Valentina hesitates, then takes it. A pulse of energy surges through her, and the lantern flares with an incandescent glow. As the sentinel takes it back, the light fades.
Is your mind in the light now?
What was that?! What kind of artifact is this?
A mirror, revealing the light within you.
Maybe I’m simply not versed in magic, but that was impressive.
Now that the matter of trust is settled, we must depart. Farewell, creatures of Alva —
Wait. I never said I was going with you. I may trust you a little more, but you expect me to abandon my whole life in an instant? Life isn’t some grand tale where the hero simply answers the call.
A… tale?
It means I decide my own path. And if there truly is some heavenly force within me, then our paths will cross again.
…Very well. We shall meet again.
For a moment, I thought you might decide to leave with her.
Would that have been a problem?
One might say so. Now, time to return to Alvar and introduce you to the Triumvirate.
Very well. Lead the way.
With a flash of light, the sentinel dissolves into nothingness.
I have waited for thee.
Who’s there?! Show yourself!
Such a capable creature, to have made it this far! Yet all that effort, wasted — serving fools and liars.
I serve Alvar. Better tell me what you are before things turn messy.
I am a messenger, bearing the will of the true king! He who loves all his subjects and welcomes those who dwell upon this cursed world.
So, you’re one of those insects? But you can talk…
We are the uplifted. We have embraced a new master, and in return, he has ascended us. You, too, can be ascended.
No need to fret over kin or kind, for they will all join the tide. Only the misbegotten tyrants who peddle the lie of kingdoms and empires shall be condemned.
Sounds like you’re just asking me to trade one master for another.
No! For ours is the only master worthy of devotion! To serve him is to love him.
Other kings rule through pacts and punishment. He has no need.
Will you listen? Will you open your heart to his words?
Alright. Let’s hear this “message” of yours.
I’ve heard enough. My loyalties aren’t for sale. Tell your master that.
You have chosen wisely. Let the words of Beelzebub take root in your soul.
[The Waurms joined your army]
How… unfortunate.
You’ve made a mistake.
I have waited for thee.
Who be there?
Elemental being, born in ways unknown. So powerful, yet so mistaken. How do you not value thy might — wasting it by serving others?
We find those we serve… interesting. And you? What might you be?

I am simply an envoy, nothing more. But there are forces guiding me, far beyond the petty worries of local rulers. I follow the true king — the one who loves all his subjects and welcomes all who dwell upon this cursed world.
You burn! You burn! You are the insects that burn! Yet you speak…
Indeed, we speak, for we are the uplifted. We have recognized the power of the new master, and in return, he has ascended us. You, the offspring of two elements, may be ascended as well.

You don’t have to abandon those you care about or think about, for there’s room in our Song for every being. Only the misbegotten tyrants who peddle the lie of kingdoms and empires shall be condemned.
So we’d just be joining a new master?
No! For ours is the master who doesn’t crave the indignity of his people! To serve him is to love him — and to receive his love in return.
Other kings rule through pacts and punishment. He has no need.
Will you listen? Will you embrace his words?
We are… interested. Speak. Let us hear your truth.
We would rather things remain as they are. Burning change… is dangerous.
Right choice indeed, the elemental wanderer. Let the words of Beelzebub take root in your soul.
[The Waurms joined your army]
It’s a shame.
You’ve chosen the wrong side.
Life so hard…
A lone wanderer, adrift in shadows. Did your kin cast you aside, sir?
No, not at all. The medusae want to feed me to their hydras! Just for sneaking in for their treasures!
Their vaults are full of treasures, but too well guarded! They’ll catch me and turn me into a hydra snack before I can leap away…
You seek riches? You need us, then!
Oh! You’re intruders, too? We can help each other! You’re too big for the tunnels, but strong enough to handle the guards!
And how much of the gleaming spoils would we claim?
Errr… Seventy‑thirty my way?
A lopsided deal. Like a river bent by the wind. You’d better make it fair, and then we might agree.
It sounds like you are trying to rob us as well. Let us show you how we deal with thieves. [Attack]
This talk is wasted breath. We don’t have time to bargain.
But it’s my plan! …M‑maybe sixty‑forty?
And yet, our army.
I… fine! Half and half! Just help me, and I’ll show you the way!
[The Troglodyte joined your army]
Wait — no help at all?!
No help, and our soldiers frown upon such brazen robbery.
W‑well… if you change your mind, I’ll be here!
There! I hear the guards — just beyond! That’s the tunnel I need!
Tear through them!
The medusae are gone. And indeed there is a small tunnel!
Told you! You’d never have fit!
And now?
Now… I go down, gather the treasure. Might take a while…
No drowning deception? No smoldering betrayal?
N‑never! I’ll be back, arms full of gold! Promise!
[Troglodyte left your army]
I’m back! Just like I promised!
[Received 4 Wood and 4 Ore]
Hm. A light offering. We expected more.
Small problem… Too much treasure! Can’t carry it all at once!
This is just the first load!
We cannot wait…
I get it! I get it! Take what I’ve got now. The rest — I’ll claim it after you’re gone!
Acceptable.
More treasures for you, partner!
[You gained the Fish‑to‑the‑Face artifact!]
Err… Excuse me? Where are we going?
To Alvar.
Wait… Something lingers, unfinished.
Oh, just great! Now I’ll never get the medusae’s treasure!
A river does not flow backward. Perhaps you’ll find a better occupation, though!
Goodbye, my life of jingling gold…
Here you go! The last of it!
[Received 10,000 Gold!]
And sadly, this is where we must part. The path ahead calls.
Guess that means the rest of the treasure is mine.
You helped me lots! Maybe we’ll meet again someday!
Stories say this is where the fabled Altars of Mearea stand. Ever heard of her?
Hm? No. A spirit of the past?
In a way. Some say she was a dragon, glistening so brightly that her shine still lingers — bringing gold and fortune to those who find her favor. Unpredictable, and very dangerous.
A horseshoe. Fortune in a curve.
The wind carries horses far and fast.
Are we feeling lucky, though?
[Touch it]
[Step away]
The symbol awakens!
[You gained the Tabar Signet‑Ring artifact!]
We are favored. What shall we do with such a gift?
Aksh! A cruel spark!
[Heroes’ Luck set to –5 until the end of the mission]
The wind turns against us.
Luck — fickle, fleeting. Let’s hope the weight of misfortune lifts soon…
The sun. A mark of light. But how in blazes do I prove mine? I’m no lantern, no candle…
[Touch it]
[Step away]
Whoa! The symbol flares to life!
[Heroes’ sight radius increased by 2 until the end of the mission]
And my vision — clear as midday! Must be tied to Daylight magic.
[Heroes’ Sight Radius reduced by 2 until the end of the mission]
Gah! My eyes — blinded!
Not worthy, huh? I’ll need a healer for this…
A round gold piece! So this is the Altar of Gold, then? A test of wealth. Does it demand a tribute?
[Touch it]
[Step away]
The symbol gleams!
[Received 10,000 Gold!]
We gave nothing. Yet gained.
Is this how the rich grow richer?
[Lost all Gold]
Vanished! Our gold — gone to embers.
Gunnar will be less than pleased…
The magic has fled this place. Now, only a husk remains — old stones, worn and silent.
Your reasoning is sssound, but the path is not as simple as you think. Many will not align with your vision.
That is acceptable. I don’t need all of you — just enough to tip the scales. Pardon the metaphor.
You again!
And who is thisss?
A timely interruption. I was preparing to leave.
Then we’ll address them.
And she’s gone…
I have a bad feeling about this.
It looks like the same portal… But it won’t open.
We’ll need another way out.
Finally, after a painstaking hunt, we have tracked down our knight.
Thank you for saving my life. Unfortunately, I have little more to add beyond what I already told your emissary.
My party was ambushed by medusae not far from Garner Hills. That’s when we learned the entire village was ablaze and the fires refused to die.

The emissary, Gunnar, mentioned you heard the Wild Ones whispering something about sacrificing you to some king. However, the renegade medusae don’t have kings or queens that we know of. Any idea who this might be?
They don’t? Hm, I hadn’t thought about it. There was mention of a “dragon‑flying‑king.” If they don’t serve a dragon king, could it be Garrote Gorge?
Those medusae definitely do not serve anyone, not even the one dragon they should obey… And Garrote Gorge? It has never had a king.
Dragon‑flying, you say? Or could it be a dragonfly?
A dragonfly — Wait… No…
Sounds like a yes. A dragonfly king would certainly fit the picture.
But you said those insects couldn’t even speak! How could they choose a king, and why would he want to kidnap me?
I suspect that’s a mystery you’ll need to solve with Gunnar and Lafiur.
Rest now, child, but not for long. If there is a fully sentient creature behind this chaos, we do not have much time left…
Ah, this place is… quite peculiar.
Peculiar?
Home‑shaped. No constraints of continuity, no tethering of spatial logic. Can’t you feel it?
…I suppose there is a certain resemblance to Lafiur’s home. Assuming their home is the Whispering World.
They say this used to be Doreath’s testing range. He tampered with time and space here. He’s long gone, but the damage remains.
Don’t be surprised if you encounter shortcuts, paradoxes, or dead ends. Doreath left those behind.
So, does this mean whoever controls the keep can find “shortcuts” to other places? Like, say, Alvar? And use them for a sneak attack?
I believe it’s possible.
Then we need to take this keep back.
Can we then stay there?
The Triumvirate will decide its fate.
Ah, we see. A pity. We wish we could linger here. We feel so free in here…
I know, Lafiur. We will help you eventually.
He wasn’t lying, but oh, how sorely he was mistaken.
He said I wasn’t powerful enough to be deemed an ally. Yet there’s only one of me — and so many of you. I’d say that speaks of my power.
Then again, a necromancer is never alone. Not as long as there are corpses to raise.
Who are you?
You said “he wasn’t lying.” Who?
Are you the one controlling those fiery insects?
Your future lord. I would say king, but unfortunately, that title is already claimed, for now.
The Dragonfly King! You’ll meet him soon enough.
Ah, yes. Vampires are known for their burning hearts.
If your allies are as foolish as you, no one will even notice when I raise you as a skeleton.
You are minions of the Triumvirate, aren’t you? Of Alvar, the city that praises individuality and open‑mindedness… yet never grasped my rising star.
Well, someone else did. Someone much stronger than you.
Do you know what’s going on with the insects or not?
And a sun‑licking girl. What a company. When I decorate my wall with your skull, I hope you won’t shine too bright.
Didn’t you plan to raise us?
Friends. We need to figure out how to open this path.
Whoa. What was that?
We believe Autumn is what you call this experience.
…What?
Seasons come in four. One fourth is now set free. Free to flow like a river. We can feel it.
They’re saying some sort of channel has opened… I think. Or rather, a fourth of it.
We should probably find this channel then. And keep unlocking it.
Rejoice! The gate is open!
Let’s do it.
Whoever enters that portal will likely face Lord Galthran. They should prepare their forces before proceeding.
It is rare that things come to me so easily. Welcome, but don’t get too comfortable. You won’t be staying long.
You might have trouble getting rid of us now that your amplifiers are gone!
So that is why my skeletons have stopped responding… you were behind that? How? Only undead are supposed to be able to operate them!
The leader of the Necromancers Guild decided to intervene on our behalf.
Although, it still wasn’t easy.
Ah, Thant. That old fool decided to crawl out of his coffin, did he?
Fine! I will deal with you without the amplifiers bolstering my strength.
All those efforts, and what for?
You mentioned the “Dragonfly King” earlier. Who is he?
We don’t want to fight. We just need answers.
A necromancer commanding insects and not the undead. How does that feel?
Didn’t Alvar’s spies tell you of the greatest force on the continent? How sad.
I am one of Alvar’s spies. That’s exactly why I’m asking.
Oh, there’s no need to fight. You could always end this peacefully… by surrendering your own life. I’d gladly raise you as a lieutenant, but you — you elemental creatures disperse too fast.
Yet, seeing your hesitation is truly inspiring.
The Dragonfly King’s forces are more than capable of dealing with all of you.
Interesting choice of words. You refer to them as his forces. Who is he? Does it frustrate you to serve someone stronger than yourself?
Hah! You think me weak?
No, I think you’re just like every other necromancer — more accustomed to controlling the dead. That’s something we can use against you.
I’ll say one thing to sate some of your curiosity. The Dragonfly King is the ruler of all that is red. Blood, fire, crystals… the Scarlet Star.
Your tiara?
Hah, no one controls me. The offer he made me… He asked me to extend it to you as well. But why would I want to create competition for myself?
I’ll simply tell him you refused, and then you’ll be long gone already.
You can’t kill me! I’m immortal! Undead — I’ve defied death once, and I’ll do it again!
I don’t plan on killing you. You’re coming with us to Alvar. You’ll speak to the Triumvirate — about the Dragonfly King, the fires, and the creatures under your command.
Once we have the answers, this ends.
The gate will open only once all four channels are activated.
More of Galthran’s minions!
Hardly. Put aside your weapons. I’m not here to fight you.
Conflict between us would only serve our common enemy. My name is Thant, and I wish to aid your band in bringing down Galthran.
Thant? The Thant? Leader of the Necromancers Guild?
The one and only! My reputation precedes me.
Why would a necromancer want to help us? Especially against one of his own?
Normally, I wouldn’t extend aid. However, Galthran was once someone I had high hopes for. It pains me to see him making such poor choices in allies.
I thought it odd that a vampire would side with fire creatures.
Demonic fire creatures, yes. It’s incomparably misguided.
These insects will devour every corpse they come across — bodies and souls alike. Nothing will be left for the necromancers to use. That’s why Galthran must be stopped.
We have our sights set on that goal. How do you intend to help?
Simple. Galthran is using my own methods to bolster his necromancy. Fighting him while he’s strengthening his power will be tricky.
You may have seen some obelisks nearby. These devices strengthen necromancy and are based on my designs. I’ll reveal their locations and tell you how to shut them down.
You’d reveal one of the Guild’s secrets, just like that?
Galthran is the bigger threat. Think of this as a gesture of goodwill toward Alvar. In return, I ask that you do not kill him permanently.

I suppose we’ll take you up on your offer.
Excellent. Here are the instructions.
mumbles Wait… what’s this?

Ah, this is where the good news ends: you don’t. The energy will harm anything that’s not undead. They were never meant to be operated by the living.
However, I have a countermeasure — a single‑use crystal that will shut down the energy. I can give it to you, but… there’s a price.
I knew it. You can’t make a deal with a necromancer without some sort of catch.
I simply want a favor in the future.
Semantics.
I think he really does want to help.
Eh. I’d rather not owe favors to someone I’ve just met.
Fine. Let’s hope we don’t regret it.

Oh? Where did the sun go?
Something tells me that Galthran is responsible…
I’ve read about this in the documents that Thant gave us. Those amplifiers will cover everything in darkness until we break them!
We sense a lot of undead inside…
Great — not only will we be subjecting ourselves to harmful energies, but these things are guarded too.
Not like we have much choice if we want to weaken Galthran.

Maybe we use the thing that Thant gave us.
Amazing! It completely shut it down and disabled all the undead within!

Another amplifier.
We already dealt with one of these. The second one should not be any more of a problem.
The last amplifier.
We are one step away from taking this Galthran down a notch.
The undead are defeated. Now comes the hard part.
We sense that this will not feel good…

One more time, then…
This had all better be worth it.

Let’s hope we will still be in good enough condition to face Galthran…
Ugh! That felt awful!

Time to go and face the bloodsucker.
A phoenix… What is it doing here?
Its life ends… We should take its egg to the Fungal Grove…

It seeks to connect with the Mycelium, to transfer its memories and make its rebirth easier. Memories are a burden; they make rebirth difficult. As for why it’s here, we can’t say.
Hrm. We’ve got more pressing matters, but… something tells me there’s a reward in this for us.
It’s for you to decide. The ashes are cool enough to extract the egg from them.
Well, as my pa used to say, never pass up a free egg!
Did your father really say that?

Might I request you surrender it to me?

I need it for my experiments. I’m fascinated by how resurrection works in these birds. Fire usually destroys everything, yet they rise from it.
For us necromancers, life, death, and undeath are key studies. But if you’re unwilling to help for the sake of research, I can always put in a good word for you with my superiors.
Well, if it’s for research… [Give Phoenix Egg]
Go find your own egg. We’re not running an egg charity.
Why don’t you use the insects for your research instead? They’re fire‑aligned too.
Your understanding was not expected, but it is welcome. With this egg, I might advance necromantic science.
We expected better from you, Gunnar.
Hey, he was hunting the thing, right? Hunters have a right to their prey.
Besides, I’m curious about the results of his experiments.
I hunted this phoenix! And you claim the prize is yours?
I regret resorting to this… I am, after all, a man of learning.
Oh, if I had a grain for every necromancer who said that, the world would never go hungry.
Not even a single grand speech?
I’d rather not watch my undead tear you apart, but it seems I have no choice.
It’s obvious why. They don’t use their fire for rebirth!
But… one of my colleagues actually had the same idea. Hah… proves how stubborn he is!
Sigh. He’s my friend, though.
Sounds like a predicament.
His insistence on his theories has driven a wedge between us. He won’t listen to me!
But… maybe you can help.
I’ll let you keep the egg if you go to my colleague and help him with his research.
Help him? Don’t you think his theories are nonsense?
Why? Don’t you think that his theories are nonsense?
Utter nonsense. But if you help him test them, they’ll fail, and he might finally see my point.
As a third party, you’re the only ones he’ll listen to.
Alright, we can do that.
Nah. Let’s just fight.
Thank you. Here are directions to where he lives.
Very well. My minions will show you no mercy.
[Phoenix Egg removed from your inventory]
We’ve done the right thing. The great bird can reconnect with the Murmurwoods’ consciousness — fire, water, all the elements.
Does that mean it’ll be reborn?
Yes, but not here.
Shame. It would have been nice to have a phoenix fighting for us.
We will have something much stronger behind us now. The might of the forest. The might of the elements.
This place feels… strange.
The connection to the Mycelium is strong here. You can feel the echoes of the Murmurwoods, both near and far.
You there, uhm… You need any help?
Mumblemumble— Blast it!
Are you even listening to me?
Hm…? Who are you?
I figured you could use a hand.
Oh, no. I’m fine.
Doesn’t sound like it. Sounds like you’re having trouble with your necromancy experiment.
Necroinsectology.
…What?
What I’m doing is not necromancy! It’s necroinsectology!
We have never heard of anything like this.
It’s… still in its infancy, a new arcane science! These creatures arrived recently, but mark my words — it will be famous soon enough! It’s just that…
Yes?
I can’t make any progress. I need a live specimen… but my attempts to summon and bind one have failed.
That’s all? We can bring you a live insect in no time.
That… that would be wonderful! I’ll be sure to reward you for your work.
We’ll be back before you know it!
whispers How exactly do you plan on bringing one of those things back alive?
whispers I have absolutely no idea.
Did you seriously think any of them would surrender?
Well this was a waste of time.
It was worth a shot, okay?
If only we were more experienced in diplomacy!
Not likely. They don’t talk.
You will have better luck convincing a skeleton.
Well, that worked.
It seems there are places where summoning these beings is possible.
No insects, no ritual!
There! One demonic insect, as ordered!

Ah, this is perfect! Now, hold it still while I prepare the ritual. This will only hurt for a moment.

Stand back! I’m about to craft an undead minion the likes of which this world has never seen before!

It looks like a regular skeleton to me…

My theories…

confused rattling

Our standoff with the humans continues. Will you aid us?

Creepy‑looking thing. Best to keep our distance.

Heh. Doreath’s handiwork. This was his testing ground. I wish I’d figured out how to use it myself, but only demons seem to make sense of its secrets.

You?!

I believe that means she is not going with you.
I will prevail where the angels failed.
We can blaze ahead! We can flow forward!
Let’s do it.
Whoever enters that portal will have a date with destiny… and Valentina. They should prepare before proceeding.

I will prevail where the angels failed!

Uuugh…

And you will tell the Triumvirate all about the Dragonfly King, the fires, and the creatures you commanded.
And we will finally know.
An angel serving a corrupted knight… Something’s not right here.
Valentina walks a path we are bound to.
We suppress doubt — that is our charge.
Valentina and we are at cross purposes.

As one of us faltered — and remains imprisoned still.
Imprisoned? Gunnar, we think it’s worth searching for this one.

Surrender.

Valentina will complete this world.

Not interested. For Alvar!

Let’s go and find this prisoner, then.

One would hope to be greeted with gratitude!

Desperation, I’d say… and a touch of magic. He saved me from being sacrificed to the hydras in the dungeons.
I won’t lie — I considered his offer for a time. But the moment I accepted it? That part is… hazy.
Apologies for the interruption, but a messenger left this for you… all of you.

The entire Archeologists Guild is gone.

Are you doubting our strength?!
Apologies, Gunnar. Of course I put my trust in Jadame’s mightiest fortress, but still…

Hmph. Can’t we just raise a legion of skeletons?…

Kneel, mortals, before Bathym, Duke of Jewels!

Record…?
Elementals cannot write.
A pity. The Murmurwoods’ creatures have a way of overlooking… opportunities.

That was… acceptable.

This can’t be it. The Dragonfly King has deceived us before, drawing our focus away. This could be another ploy.

I…
